ACTIVITIES:

Oxbow Corporation, founded in 1983 by William I. Koch in West Palm Beach, Florida, is a privately held energy and commodities company. Specializing in petroleum coke, coal, and carbon products, Oxbow generates over $4 billion annually, operating in 40 countries. With 1,200 employees, its subsidiaries, like Oxbow Carbon, focus on calcined coke for aluminum and steel industries. Oxbows logistics network ensures efficient global distribution. The company invests in renewable energy and carbon management, aligning with sustainability trends. Despite legal disputes, including a $1 billion tax settlement in 2021, Oxbow remains a key player in energy markets, leveraging innovation and strategic partnerships to drive growth.

+ OXBOW [3 News That Matters]

+ OXBOW BREWING'S LEADERSHIP SHIFT: A NEW ERA UNDER BRI WARNER

Oxbow Brewing Company is undergoing a significant leadership transition as Bri Warner, the new president, assumes a more active role in the brewery's day-to-day operations. As of 18th March 2026, Warner is just two months into her position, allowing founder Tim Adams to concentrate on promoting Oxbow and its recently acquired brand, Rising Tide.Warner, who previously worked at Atlantic Sea Farms, the largest kelp farming operation in the United States, brings a fresh perspective to Oxbow Brewing. In a recent podcast recorded at Oxbow’s Portland taproom, Adams expressed his confidence in Warner’s capabilities, stating, "Bri just is an absolute master at doing that." This transition is seen as essential for the company's long-term sustainability, particularly as it manages multiple brands and locations.With Warner at the forefront of operations, Adams has been able to step back from daily management and focus on brand representation. This winter, he has spent considerable time at Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford, Maine, engaging with trade partners and consumers while overseeing the brewery's various offerings in a picturesque setting. Warner noted, "What that does is it keeps Tim from being out on the road, and I think probably the most power of this is the fact that we get to free him to do the work to continue to grow the brand in a really great and passionate way."The leadership change comes at a time when many breweries face challenges in balancing operational demands with brand growth. Warner aims to bridge this gap at Oxbow Brewing, leveraging her expertise to tighten internal operations while maintaining the brewery's focus on quality and creativity. She commented, "I think that you see a lot of breweries and a lot of people in the industry that are having a hard time bridging that gap, and hopefully this move will allow that to do that."As Oxbow Brewing navigates this new chapter, the industry is watching closely. The podcast also covered other recent developments in the beer sector, including Tilray’s acquisition of BrewDog’s Australian operations and the launch of George Clooney’s new non-alcoholic brand. With strategic leadership in place, Oxbow Brewing is poised for continued growth and innovation.

+ OXBOW RESORT EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH BERKSHIRE PIZZERIA ACQUISITION

CHARLEMONT — Oxbow Resort owner Travis DeCere has expanded his business interests by acquiring Berkshire Pizzeria and the former Otters restaurant, marking a significant investment in the local dining scene. DeCere, who has been renovating the Oxbow Resort Motel for over a year, solidified his plans on 8th February 2026, finalizing the purchase of the pizzeria for $330,000 through Berkshire Equity LLC.During his frequent trips to Charlemont for the Oxbow renovations, DeCere connected with Greg Rowehl, the previous owner of Berkshire Pizzeria. Initially focused on the motel, DeCere found himself intrigued by the restaurant opportunity. Now, with the motel nearing completion and extended-stay tenants occupying the back buildings, he is ready to embrace this new challenge alongside his business partner, Colleen Brady.Brady, who has relocated to Charlemont from Florida, brings substantial culinary experience, having owned food trucks in the past. She plans to introduce several changes to the pizzeria, including an expanded menu featuring fresh chicken tenders and potential pasta options. The duo aims to enhance the restaurant's ambiance and offerings, including a full bar on the back deck, in hopes of attracting more customers, especially skiers from Berkshire East Mountain Resort.In addition to the pizzeria, DeCere also acquired the former Otters restaurant property for $250,000 and plans to find a tenant to create a full-service restaurant. The Otters property, located at 1745 Route 2, provides ample space with a full kitchen and bar, and is currently listed for lease on commercial real estate platforms. This strategic acquisition represents a commitment to enriching the culinary landscape of Charlemont while building upon the legacy established by the previous owners of the pizzeria.As Oxbow Resort continues to evolve, DeCere and Brady are focused on honoring the legacy of the Rowehl family while also injecting their own vision into the local dining experience. With plans to refresh the pizzeria's interior and expand its operational hours, they aim to create a community hub that reflects the charm of Charlemont.

+ OXBOW BREWING COMPANY APPOINTS BRIANA WARNER AS NEW PRESIDENT

PORTLAND, MAINE - Oxbow Brewing Company has announced the appointment of Briana Warner as President, marking a significant leadership change for the brewery. Since its beginnings in Newcastle, Maine, Oxbow has grown with patience and purpose, guided by a deep respect for craft, community, and place. The announcement was made on the 11th of February 2026, as the company looks toward its next chapter under Warner's leadership.Warner, who previously served as CEO of Atlantic Sea Farms and has an extensive background as a U.S. diplomat, brings a wealth of experience in scaling mission-driven businesses while keeping community values at the forefront. Her tenure at Atlantic Sea Farms saw the company evolve into the largest farmed kelp operation in the United States, establishing a nationally recognized brand rooted in Maine's working waterfront. As President of Oxbow Brewing Company, she will be responsible for leading company strategy, overseeing daily operations, and guiding the brewery's growth across its wholesale and hospitality sectors.Tim Adams, the Founder of Oxbow Brewing Company, will continue to play an active role within the company, focusing on brand development, creative direction, sales, and community engagement. 'Briana is an outstanding leader and a natural fit for Oxbow,' said Adams. 'Her experience and values align perfectly with our commitment to thoughtful, sustainable growth.' This leadership evolution comes at a pivotal time for Oxbow, which has seen its distribution extend well beyond its original farmhouse brewery in Newcastle.The brewery operates two taprooms and event spaces in Portland, along with the Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford—a year-round destination that combines wood-fired, farm-driven food with outdoor recreation. The Oxford campus features groomed ski and snowshoe trails in winter and an 18-hole disc golf course during warmer months, highlighting the brewery's commitment to community engagement and seasonal menus that showcase organic produce grown on-site.Oxbow Brewing Company's recent acquisition of Rising Tide Brewing Company has further solidified its standing in the Maine craft beer scene. This strategic partnership has enhanced operational efficiencies and expanded production capabilities while maintaining independent ownership of both brands. Warner's appointment is seen as a vital step in ensuring long-term stability and growth for Oxbow and its newly acquired partner, reinforcing the brewery's commitment to producing high-quality, balanced beers.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY PARTNERS WITH MICROSOFT IN AI POWER INITIATIVE

Constellation Energy is partnering with Microsoft to restart the Three Mile Island nuclear facility, aiming to supply power specifically for data centers. This strategic collaboration aligns with growing demands for carbon-free electricity in the rapidly expanding artificial intelligence sector. The agreement represents a significant shift in how energy is being sourced for digital infrastructure, with the potential for substantial implications in the clean energy landscape.The partnership with Microsoft, announced on 28th April 2026, positions Constellation Energy at the forefront of AI power sourcing discussions, especially as hyperscale data center operators increasingly seek reliable, carbon-free baseload power. This move not only enhances Constellation's existing generation portfolio but also adds a high-profile customer that reflects the energy demands of today’s tech-driven economy. The share price of Constellation Energy recently stood at $315.17, reflecting a notable increase of 40.9% over the past year.This deal dovetails with Constellation Energy's broader strategy of entering into large, contracted clean-power agreements. By tying a legacy nuclear asset to the burgeoning demand from AI-driven applications, the company is set to benefit from long-term visibility on volumes and pricing, particularly if the partnership is structured as a multi-year agreement. Analysts suggest that the implications of this agreement may alter the dynamics of nuclear energy discussions in the context of the clean energy transition.Investors should remain vigilant regarding the structure of the contracts associated with the Three Mile Island facility, including the duration and pricing mechanisms, as well as any commitments related to 24/7 carbon-free energy. The partnership could influence the competitive landscape, particularly as tech giants like Meta and other cloud providers weigh their options for similar agreements with energy suppliers. Moreover, the move comes at a time when Constellation Energy has seen a resurgence in stock price, despite a year-to-date decline of 13.9%.In the coming months, stakeholders will want to track updates on regulatory approvals and capital requirements for the Three Mile Island project. The successful execution of this plan, along with the integration of the recent Calpine acquisition and ongoing partnerships, will be crucial for Constellation Energy's growth trajectory. The company's ability to replicate these types of agreements with additional data center customers could further solidify its position in the clean energy market.

+ NRG ENERGY [3 News That Matters]

+ NRG ENERGY SECURES $2.6 BILLION THROUGH STRATEGIC DEBT OFFERINGS

HOUSTON - NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) has successfully priced a substantial $2.6 billion in debt offerings across three distinct tranches, as announced in a recent statement. This strategic move comes on the heels of the decision made on 17th April 2026 to enhance the company's liquidity and refinance existing obligations.The offerings comprise $500 million in 4.955% senior secured first lien notes due 2031, alongside $1.05 billion in 5.875% senior unsecured notes due 2034 and another $1.05 billion in 6.125% senior unsecured notes due 2036. The new notes will be guaranteed by NRG Energy's current and future wholly-owned U.S. subsidiaries, providing additional security for investors. The secured notes are backed by a first priority security interest in collateral already pledged for NRG's existing credit agreements and senior secured notes.As of the end of the fourth quarter of 2025, NRG Energy's total debt stood at $16.6 billion. However, the company maintains a robust current ratio of 1.64, indicating that its liquid assets comfortably exceed short-term obligations. The net proceeds from the current debt offerings will be utilized to repay a portion of outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility and fund the tender price for a previously announced offer by its subsidiary Lightning Power, LLC for outstanding senior secured notes. Any residual proceeds may be allocated for general corporate purposes, including the repayment of other debts.The completion of each of the offerings is independent, with no conditions tied to the other offerings or the term loan B. The notes are being offered exclusively to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A and to non-U.S. persons outside the United States under Regulation S, with no registration under the Securities Act of 1933. This move highlights NRG Energy's commitment to optimizing its capital structure amidst a dynamic market environment.In related news, NRG Energy's subsidiary Lightning Power has initiated a tender offer to purchase up to $1.5 billion in senior secured notes due 2032, offering $1,063.75 per $1,000 principal amount for notes tendered by 27th April 2026, including an early tender payment. Analysts have responded positively to NRG's strategic maneuvers, with Jefferies raising its price target to $199 and Wolfe Research upgrading the stock to Outperform, both citing the anticipated growth in the sector. Goldman Sachs has also reinstated coverage with a Buy rating, emphasizing the positive impact of NRG's recent acquisitions, which are expected to bolster its business profile moving forward.

+ NRG ENERGY UNVEILS NEW OFFERINGS OF SECURED AND UNSECURED NOTES

NRG Energy, Inc. has announced the commencement of concurrent offerings of senior secured notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036. On 14th April 2026, the company revealed that these notes will be guaranteed by its U.S. subsidiaries, with the secured notes backed by a first-priority lien on substantially all assets pledged under its existing credit facilities.The proceeds from these offerings are intended to be utilized in conjunction with a proposed $900 million term loan B, aimed primarily at repaying borrowings under NRG Energy's revolving credit facility. Additionally, the funds will support a tender offer for Lightning Power’s 7.25% senior secured notes, which are due in 2032. NRG Energy emphasizes that any remaining proceeds will be allocated towards paying fees and expenses, as well as general corporate purposes, including potential debt repayment.The announcement has been positively received in the market, as evidenced by a 3.76% increase in NRG Energy's shares following the news. Investors are keenly watching the company's financial maneuvers, particularly as it seeks to optimize its capital structure and enhance liquidity through these note offerings.This strategic move comes at a time when NRG Energy is focusing on strengthening its balance sheet and positioning itself for future growth opportunities. The successful execution of these offerings could provide the company with the necessary resources to manage its debt effectively while pursuing its operational goals.As NRG Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy landscape, the market will be attentive to how these financial strategies play out. Investors are encouraged to monitor the company's developments closely, as the implications of this notes offering could significantly impact its long-term financial health.

+ NRG ENERGY TARGETS DEBT MARKETS WITH NEW OFFERINGS AND $900 MILLION LOAN

NRG Energy, Inc. is making significant strides in the debt markets with a multi-tranche offering aimed at refinancing existing obligations and extending maturities. On 14th April 2026, NRG Energy announced the launch of concurrent offerings of senior secured first-lien notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036.The proceeds from these offerings, along with a new $900 million term loan B, will primarily be utilized to repay borrowings under NRG’s revolving credit facility and to fund a tender offer for outstanding 7.25% senior secured notes due 2032, which were issued by its subsidiary Lightning Power LLC. Additional funds may be allocated for general corporate purposes, including further debt reduction.The secured notes will be backed by a first-priority lien on a substantial portion of NRG's assets, mirroring collateral pledged under its existing credit facilities and secured notes. In contrast, the unsecured notes will rank behind the secured debt, offering investors a higher yield in exchange for increased risk — a choice that reflects the current appetite for yield among fixed-income investors.This transaction is indicative of a broader refinancing trend among U.S. independent power producers, many of whom are seeking to term out near-dated liabilities and lower funding costs following a period of elevated interest rates. The energy sector is undergoing a transformation as companies like NRG Energy position themselves for a surge in electricity demand driven by burgeoning data centers and electrification initiatives.As NRG Energy continues to navigate these dynamic market conditions, its latest debt offerings could attract fresh investor interest, particularly as power infrastructure becomes increasingly critical in the age of artificial intelligence. The steps taken by NRG demonstrate a proactive approach in optimizing its capital structure and extending financial flexibility.

DASHBOARD: OXBOW

PermID4296883232Websitehttps://www.oxbow.com/IndustryOil and Gas

ACTIVITIES:

Oxbow Corporation, founded in 1983 by William I. Koch in West Palm Beach, Florida, is a privately held energy and commodities company. Specializing in petroleum coke, coal, and carbon products, Oxbow generates over $4 billion annually, operating in 40 countries. With 1,200 employees, its subsidiaries, like Oxbow Carbon, focus on calcined coke for aluminum and steel industries. Oxbows logistics network ensures efficient global distribution. The company invests in renewable energy and carbon management, aligning with sustainability trends. Despite legal disputes, including a $1 billion tax settlement in 2021, Oxbow remains a key player in energy markets, leveraging innovation and strategic partnerships to drive growth.

+ OXBOW [3 News That Matters]

+ OXBOW BREWING'S LEADERSHIP SHIFT: A NEW ERA UNDER BRI WARNER

Oxbow Brewing Company is undergoing a significant leadership transition as Bri Warner, the new president, assumes a more active role in the brewery's day-to-day operations. As of 18th March 2026, Warner is just two months into her position, allowing founder Tim Adams to concentrate on promoting Oxbow and its recently acquired brand, Rising Tide.Warner, who previously worked at Atlantic Sea Farms, the largest kelp farming operation in the United States, brings a fresh perspective to Oxbow Brewing. In a recent podcast recorded at Oxbow’s Portland taproom, Adams expressed his confidence in Warner’s capabilities, stating, "Bri just is an absolute master at doing that." This transition is seen as essential for the company's long-term sustainability, particularly as it manages multiple brands and locations.With Warner at the forefront of operations, Adams has been able to step back from daily management and focus on brand representation. This winter, he has spent considerable time at Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford, Maine, engaging with trade partners and consumers while overseeing the brewery's various offerings in a picturesque setting. Warner noted, "What that does is it keeps Tim from being out on the road, and I think probably the most power of this is the fact that we get to free him to do the work to continue to grow the brand in a really great and passionate way."The leadership change comes at a time when many breweries face challenges in balancing operational demands with brand growth. Warner aims to bridge this gap at Oxbow Brewing, leveraging her expertise to tighten internal operations while maintaining the brewery's focus on quality and creativity. She commented, "I think that you see a lot of breweries and a lot of people in the industry that are having a hard time bridging that gap, and hopefully this move will allow that to do that."As Oxbow Brewing navigates this new chapter, the industry is watching closely. The podcast also covered other recent developments in the beer sector, including Tilray’s acquisition of BrewDog’s Australian operations and the launch of George Clooney’s new non-alcoholic brand. With strategic leadership in place, Oxbow Brewing is poised for continued growth and innovation.

+ OXBOW RESORT EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH BERKSHIRE PIZZERIA ACQUISITION

CHARLEMONT — Oxbow Resort owner Travis DeCere has expanded his business interests by acquiring Berkshire Pizzeria and the former Otters restaurant, marking a significant investment in the local dining scene. DeCere, who has been renovating the Oxbow Resort Motel for over a year, solidified his plans on 8th February 2026, finalizing the purchase of the pizzeria for $330,000 through Berkshire Equity LLC.During his frequent trips to Charlemont for the Oxbow renovations, DeCere connected with Greg Rowehl, the previous owner of Berkshire Pizzeria. Initially focused on the motel, DeCere found himself intrigued by the restaurant opportunity. Now, with the motel nearing completion and extended-stay tenants occupying the back buildings, he is ready to embrace this new challenge alongside his business partner, Colleen Brady.Brady, who has relocated to Charlemont from Florida, brings substantial culinary experience, having owned food trucks in the past. She plans to introduce several changes to the pizzeria, including an expanded menu featuring fresh chicken tenders and potential pasta options. The duo aims to enhance the restaurant's ambiance and offerings, including a full bar on the back deck, in hopes of attracting more customers, especially skiers from Berkshire East Mountain Resort.In addition to the pizzeria, DeCere also acquired the former Otters restaurant property for $250,000 and plans to find a tenant to create a full-service restaurant. The Otters property, located at 1745 Route 2, provides ample space with a full kitchen and bar, and is currently listed for lease on commercial real estate platforms. This strategic acquisition represents a commitment to enriching the culinary landscape of Charlemont while building upon the legacy established by the previous owners of the pizzeria.As Oxbow Resort continues to evolve, DeCere and Brady are focused on honoring the legacy of the Rowehl family while also injecting their own vision into the local dining experience. With plans to refresh the pizzeria's interior and expand its operational hours, they aim to create a community hub that reflects the charm of Charlemont.

+ OXBOW BREWING COMPANY APPOINTS BRIANA WARNER AS NEW PRESIDENT

PORTLAND, MAINE - Oxbow Brewing Company has announced the appointment of Briana Warner as President, marking a significant leadership change for the brewery. Since its beginnings in Newcastle, Maine, Oxbow has grown with patience and purpose, guided by a deep respect for craft, community, and place. The announcement was made on the 11th of February 2026, as the company looks toward its next chapter under Warner's leadership.Warner, who previously served as CEO of Atlantic Sea Farms and has an extensive background as a U.S. diplomat, brings a wealth of experience in scaling mission-driven businesses while keeping community values at the forefront. Her tenure at Atlantic Sea Farms saw the company evolve into the largest farmed kelp operation in the United States, establishing a nationally recognized brand rooted in Maine's working waterfront. As President of Oxbow Brewing Company, she will be responsible for leading company strategy, overseeing daily operations, and guiding the brewery's growth across its wholesale and hospitality sectors.Tim Adams, the Founder of Oxbow Brewing Company, will continue to play an active role within the company, focusing on brand development, creative direction, sales, and community engagement. 'Briana is an outstanding leader and a natural fit for Oxbow,' said Adams. 'Her experience and values align perfectly with our commitment to thoughtful, sustainable growth.' This leadership evolution comes at a pivotal time for Oxbow, which has seen its distribution extend well beyond its original farmhouse brewery in Newcastle.The brewery operates two taprooms and event spaces in Portland, along with the Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford—a year-round destination that combines wood-fired, farm-driven food with outdoor recreation. The Oxford campus features groomed ski and snowshoe trails in winter and an 18-hole disc golf course during warmer months, highlighting the brewery's commitment to community engagement and seasonal menus that showcase organic produce grown on-site.Oxbow Brewing Company's recent acquisition of Rising Tide Brewing Company has further solidified its standing in the Maine craft beer scene. This strategic partnership has enhanced operational efficiencies and expanded production capabilities while maintaining independent ownership of both brands. Warner's appointment is seen as a vital step in ensuring long-term stability and growth for Oxbow and its newly acquired partner, reinforcing the brewery's commitment to producing high-quality, balanced beers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY PARTNERS WITH MICROSOFT IN AI POWER INITIATIVE

Constellation Energy is partnering with Microsoft to restart the Three Mile Island nuclear facility, aiming to supply power specifically for data centers. This strategic collaboration aligns with growing demands for carbon-free electricity in the rapidly expanding artificial intelligence sector. The agreement represents a significant shift in how energy is being sourced for digital infrastructure, with the potential for substantial implications in the clean energy landscape.The partnership with Microsoft, announced on 28th April 2026, positions Constellation Energy at the forefront of AI power sourcing discussions, especially as hyperscale data center operators increasingly seek reliable, carbon-free baseload power. This move not only enhances Constellation's existing generation portfolio but also adds a high-profile customer that reflects the energy demands of today’s tech-driven economy. The share price of Constellation Energy recently stood at $315.17, reflecting a notable increase of 40.9% over the past year.This deal dovetails with Constellation Energy's broader strategy of entering into large, contracted clean-power agreements. By tying a legacy nuclear asset to the burgeoning demand from AI-driven applications, the company is set to benefit from long-term visibility on volumes and pricing, particularly if the partnership is structured as a multi-year agreement. Analysts suggest that the implications of this agreement may alter the dynamics of nuclear energy discussions in the context of the clean energy transition.Investors should remain vigilant regarding the structure of the contracts associated with the Three Mile Island facility, including the duration and pricing mechanisms, as well as any commitments related to 24/7 carbon-free energy. The partnership could influence the competitive landscape, particularly as tech giants like Meta and other cloud providers weigh their options for similar agreements with energy suppliers. Moreover, the move comes at a time when Constellation Energy has seen a resurgence in stock price, despite a year-to-date decline of 13.9%.In the coming months, stakeholders will want to track updates on regulatory approvals and capital requirements for the Three Mile Island project. The successful execution of this plan, along with the integration of the recent Calpine acquisition and ongoing partnerships, will be crucial for Constellation Energy's growth trajectory. The company's ability to replicate these types of agreements with additional data center customers could further solidify its position in the clean energy market.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY FACES PRESSURE AS ANALYSTS ADJUST PRICE TARGETS

Shares of Constellation Energy (NASDAQ: CEG) are experiencing a notable decline today, driven by an analyst's recent downward adjustment of the stock's price target. As of 2:54 p.m. ET, Constellation Energy's stock is down 3.1%, continuing a downward trend that began after the company closed at $299.14 last Thursday. This decline follows the announcement made on 23rd April 2026 regarding the revised price forecast.Morgan Stanley, while maintaining a 'buy' rating on Constellation Energy, has lowered its price target to $360 from a previous $385. This adjustment has raised concerns among investors, although the new target still suggests a potential upside of approximately 25.2% from the stock's last closing price. The adjustment reflects a more cautious outlook but does not indicate a complete lack of confidence in the company's long-term prospects.Analysts have pointed out that while price targets can influence market sentiment, the fundamental performance of a company should be the primary focus for investors. Constellation Energy has been involved in nuclear energy, a sector that has seen fluctuating dynamics due to regulatory changes and the increasing demand for clean energy solutions. Investors are advised to weigh these factors alongside analyst projections when considering their positions in the stock.Despite the current volatility, some analysts remain optimistic about Constellation Energy's long-term growth potential. The company's commitment to expanding its renewable energy portfolio and reducing carbon emissions aligns with broader energy sector trends. This strategic direction could position Constellation Energy favorably as global energy policies increasingly favor sustainability.In conclusion, while the downward revision of the price target for Constellation Energy may cause short-term fluctuations in stock performance, investors are encouraged to consider the underlying fundamentals and industry trends. The current market reaction underscores the importance of a comprehensive analysis when making investment decisions in the energy sector.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION (CEG) DEFIES MARKET TRENDS WITH POSITIVE GAINS

Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) ended the recent trading session at $292.77, reflecting a +1.95% change from the preceding day's closing price. This upward movement outpaced the S&P 500's daily loss of 0.41%. On the same day, the Dow experienced a decline of 0.36%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq saw a more substantial drop of 0.89%.Despite this daily gain, shares of Constellation Energy Corporation have depreciated by 5.33% over the past month, trailing the Oils-Energy sector's loss of 0.95% and the S&P 500's strong gain of 9.71%. Market analysts and investors are now pivoting their attention to the upcoming earnings disclosure for Constellation Energy, scheduled for May 11, 2026, with expectations pointing towards an earnings report of $2.52 per share. This figure would signify a year-over-year growth of 17.76%, while revenue forecasts suggest $8.77 billion, marking a significant 29.22% increase compared to the same quarter last year.Looking ahead, full-year projections from the Zacks Consensus Estimates indicate earnings of $11.78 per share and revenue of $32.63 billion for Constellation Energy Corporation, reflecting changes of +25.45% and +27.81%, respectively, from the prior year. Investors are advised to monitor any recent adjustments to analyst forecasts, as these revisions often indicate shifts in near-term business trends. Upward revisions in estimates typically signal a positive outlook on Constellation Energy's operational capabilities and profitability.To navigate these changes effectively, investors can leverage the Zacks Rank, a proprietary model that incorporates estimate changes to provide actionable ratings. The Zacks Rank system, which ranges from #1 (Strong Buy) to #5 (Strong Sell), has demonstrated an impressive track record of outperformance, with #1 ranked stocks yielding an average annual return of +25% since 1988. Over the last 30 days, the consensus EPS projection for Constellation Energy Corporation has increased by 1.32%, and the company currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).From a valuation perspective, Constellation Energy Corporation is trading at a Forward P/E ratio of 24.38, higher than the industry average of 16.55, suggesting a premium valuation. Additionally, CEG's PEG ratio stands at 1.13, considerably lower than the industry average of 2.52. The Alternative Energy - Other industry, part of the Oils-Energy sector, holds a favorable Zacks Industry Rank of 72, placing it among the top 30% of over 250 industries, indicating potential for future growth. Investors are encouraged to utilize Zacks.com for tracking stock-moving metrics and other relevant data in the upcoming trading sessions.

+ NRG ENERGY [3 News That Matters]

+ NRG ENERGY SECURES $2.6 BILLION THROUGH STRATEGIC DEBT OFFERINGS

HOUSTON - NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) has successfully priced a substantial $2.6 billion in debt offerings across three distinct tranches, as announced in a recent statement. This strategic move comes on the heels of the decision made on 17th April 2026 to enhance the company's liquidity and refinance existing obligations.The offerings comprise $500 million in 4.955% senior secured first lien notes due 2031, alongside $1.05 billion in 5.875% senior unsecured notes due 2034 and another $1.05 billion in 6.125% senior unsecured notes due 2036. The new notes will be guaranteed by NRG Energy's current and future wholly-owned U.S. subsidiaries, providing additional security for investors. The secured notes are backed by a first priority security interest in collateral already pledged for NRG's existing credit agreements and senior secured notes.As of the end of the fourth quarter of 2025, NRG Energy's total debt stood at $16.6 billion. However, the company maintains a robust current ratio of 1.64, indicating that its liquid assets comfortably exceed short-term obligations. The net proceeds from the current debt offerings will be utilized to repay a portion of outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility and fund the tender price for a previously announced offer by its subsidiary Lightning Power, LLC for outstanding senior secured notes. Any residual proceeds may be allocated for general corporate purposes, including the repayment of other debts.The completion of each of the offerings is independent, with no conditions tied to the other offerings or the term loan B. The notes are being offered exclusively to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A and to non-U.S. persons outside the United States under Regulation S, with no registration under the Securities Act of 1933. This move highlights NRG Energy's commitment to optimizing its capital structure amidst a dynamic market environment.In related news, NRG Energy's subsidiary Lightning Power has initiated a tender offer to purchase up to $1.5 billion in senior secured notes due 2032, offering $1,063.75 per $1,000 principal amount for notes tendered by 27th April 2026, including an early tender payment. Analysts have responded positively to NRG's strategic maneuvers, with Jefferies raising its price target to $199 and Wolfe Research upgrading the stock to Outperform, both citing the anticipated growth in the sector. Goldman Sachs has also reinstated coverage with a Buy rating, emphasizing the positive impact of NRG's recent acquisitions, which are expected to bolster its business profile moving forward.

+ NRG ENERGY UNVEILS NEW OFFERINGS OF SECURED AND UNSECURED NOTES

NRG Energy, Inc. has announced the commencement of concurrent offerings of senior secured notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036. On 14th April 2026, the company revealed that these notes will be guaranteed by its U.S. subsidiaries, with the secured notes backed by a first-priority lien on substantially all assets pledged under its existing credit facilities.The proceeds from these offerings are intended to be utilized in conjunction with a proposed $900 million term loan B, aimed primarily at repaying borrowings under NRG Energy's revolving credit facility. Additionally, the funds will support a tender offer for Lightning Power’s 7.25% senior secured notes, which are due in 2032. NRG Energy emphasizes that any remaining proceeds will be allocated towards paying fees and expenses, as well as general corporate purposes, including potential debt repayment.The announcement has been positively received in the market, as evidenced by a 3.76% increase in NRG Energy's shares following the news. Investors are keenly watching the company's financial maneuvers, particularly as it seeks to optimize its capital structure and enhance liquidity through these note offerings.This strategic move comes at a time when NRG Energy is focusing on strengthening its balance sheet and positioning itself for future growth opportunities. The successful execution of these offerings could provide the company with the necessary resources to manage its debt effectively while pursuing its operational goals.As NRG Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy landscape, the market will be attentive to how these financial strategies play out. Investors are encouraged to monitor the company's developments closely, as the implications of this notes offering could significantly impact its long-term financial health.

+ NRG ENERGY TARGETS DEBT MARKETS WITH NEW OFFERINGS AND $900 MILLION LOAN

NRG Energy, Inc. is making significant strides in the debt markets with a multi-tranche offering aimed at refinancing existing obligations and extending maturities. On 14th April 2026, NRG Energy announced the launch of concurrent offerings of senior secured first-lien notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036.The proceeds from these offerings, along with a new $900 million term loan B, will primarily be utilized to repay borrowings under NRG’s revolving credit facility and to fund a tender offer for outstanding 7.25% senior secured notes due 2032, which were issued by its subsidiary Lightning Power LLC. Additional funds may be allocated for general corporate purposes, including further debt reduction.The secured notes will be backed by a first-priority lien on a substantial portion of NRG's assets, mirroring collateral pledged under its existing credit facilities and secured notes. In contrast, the unsecured notes will rank behind the secured debt, offering investors a higher yield in exchange for increased risk — a choice that reflects the current appetite for yield among fixed-income investors.This transaction is indicative of a broader refinancing trend among U.S. independent power producers, many of whom are seeking to term out near-dated liabilities and lower funding costs following a period of elevated interest rates. The energy sector is undergoing a transformation as companies like NRG Energy position themselves for a surge in electricity demand driven by burgeoning data centers and electrification initiatives.As NRG Energy continues to navigate these dynamic market conditions, its latest debt offerings could attract fresh investor interest, particularly as power infrastructure becomes increasingly critical in the age of artificial intelligence. The steps taken by NRG demonstrate a proactive approach in optimizing its capital structure and extending financial flexibility.

DASHBOARD: OXBOW

PermID4296883232Websitehttps://www.oxbow.com/IndustryOil and Gas

ACTIVITIES:

Oxbow Corporation, founded in 1983 by William I. Koch in West Palm Beach, Florida, is a privately held energy and commodities company. Specializing in petroleum coke, coal, and carbon products, Oxbow generates over $4 billion annually, operating in 40 countries. With 1,200 employees, its subsidiaries, like Oxbow Carbon, focus on calcined coke for aluminum and steel industries. Oxbows logistics network ensures efficient global distribution. The company invests in renewable energy and carbon management, aligning with sustainability trends. Despite legal disputes, including a $1 billion tax settlement in 2021, Oxbow remains a key player in energy markets, leveraging innovation and strategic partnerships to drive growth.

+ OXBOW [3 News That Matters]

+ OXBOW BREWING'S LEADERSHIP SHIFT: A NEW ERA UNDER BRI WARNER

Oxbow Brewing Company is undergoing a significant leadership transition as Bri Warner, the new president, assumes a more active role in the brewery's day-to-day operations. As of 18th March 2026, Warner is just two months into her position, allowing founder Tim Adams to concentrate on promoting Oxbow and its recently acquired brand, Rising Tide.Warner, who previously worked at Atlantic Sea Farms, the largest kelp farming operation in the United States, brings a fresh perspective to Oxbow Brewing. In a recent podcast recorded at Oxbow’s Portland taproom, Adams expressed his confidence in Warner’s capabilities, stating, "Bri just is an absolute master at doing that." This transition is seen as essential for the company's long-term sustainability, particularly as it manages multiple brands and locations.With Warner at the forefront of operations, Adams has been able to step back from daily management and focus on brand representation. This winter, he has spent considerable time at Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford, Maine, engaging with trade partners and consumers while overseeing the brewery's various offerings in a picturesque setting. Warner noted, "What that does is it keeps Tim from being out on the road, and I think probably the most power of this is the fact that we get to free him to do the work to continue to grow the brand in a really great and passionate way."The leadership change comes at a time when many breweries face challenges in balancing operational demands with brand growth. Warner aims to bridge this gap at Oxbow Brewing, leveraging her expertise to tighten internal operations while maintaining the brewery's focus on quality and creativity. She commented, "I think that you see a lot of breweries and a lot of people in the industry that are having a hard time bridging that gap, and hopefully this move will allow that to do that."As Oxbow Brewing navigates this new chapter, the industry is watching closely. The podcast also covered other recent developments in the beer sector, including Tilray’s acquisition of BrewDog’s Australian operations and the launch of George Clooney’s new non-alcoholic brand. With strategic leadership in place, Oxbow Brewing is poised for continued growth and innovation.

+ OXBOW RESORT EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH BERKSHIRE PIZZERIA ACQUISITION

CHARLEMONT — Oxbow Resort owner Travis DeCere has expanded his business interests by acquiring Berkshire Pizzeria and the former Otters restaurant, marking a significant investment in the local dining scene. DeCere, who has been renovating the Oxbow Resort Motel for over a year, solidified his plans on 8th February 2026, finalizing the purchase of the pizzeria for $330,000 through Berkshire Equity LLC.During his frequent trips to Charlemont for the Oxbow renovations, DeCere connected with Greg Rowehl, the previous owner of Berkshire Pizzeria. Initially focused on the motel, DeCere found himself intrigued by the restaurant opportunity. Now, with the motel nearing completion and extended-stay tenants occupying the back buildings, he is ready to embrace this new challenge alongside his business partner, Colleen Brady.Brady, who has relocated to Charlemont from Florida, brings substantial culinary experience, having owned food trucks in the past. She plans to introduce several changes to the pizzeria, including an expanded menu featuring fresh chicken tenders and potential pasta options. The duo aims to enhance the restaurant's ambiance and offerings, including a full bar on the back deck, in hopes of attracting more customers, especially skiers from Berkshire East Mountain Resort.In addition to the pizzeria, DeCere also acquired the former Otters restaurant property for $250,000 and plans to find a tenant to create a full-service restaurant. The Otters property, located at 1745 Route 2, provides ample space with a full kitchen and bar, and is currently listed for lease on commercial real estate platforms. This strategic acquisition represents a commitment to enriching the culinary landscape of Charlemont while building upon the legacy established by the previous owners of the pizzeria.As Oxbow Resort continues to evolve, DeCere and Brady are focused on honoring the legacy of the Rowehl family while also injecting their own vision into the local dining experience. With plans to refresh the pizzeria's interior and expand its operational hours, they aim to create a community hub that reflects the charm of Charlemont.

+ OXBOW BREWING COMPANY APPOINTS BRIANA WARNER AS NEW PRESIDENT

PORTLAND, MAINE - Oxbow Brewing Company has announced the appointment of Briana Warner as President, marking a significant leadership change for the brewery. Since its beginnings in Newcastle, Maine, Oxbow has grown with patience and purpose, guided by a deep respect for craft, community, and place. The announcement was made on the 11th of February 2026, as the company looks toward its next chapter under Warner's leadership.Warner, who previously served as CEO of Atlantic Sea Farms and has an extensive background as a U.S. diplomat, brings a wealth of experience in scaling mission-driven businesses while keeping community values at the forefront. Her tenure at Atlantic Sea Farms saw the company evolve into the largest farmed kelp operation in the United States, establishing a nationally recognized brand rooted in Maine's working waterfront. As President of Oxbow Brewing Company, she will be responsible for leading company strategy, overseeing daily operations, and guiding the brewery's growth across its wholesale and hospitality sectors.Tim Adams, the Founder of Oxbow Brewing Company, will continue to play an active role within the company, focusing on brand development, creative direction, sales, and community engagement. 'Briana is an outstanding leader and a natural fit for Oxbow,' said Adams. 'Her experience and values align perfectly with our commitment to thoughtful, sustainable growth.' This leadership evolution comes at a pivotal time for Oxbow, which has seen its distribution extend well beyond its original farmhouse brewery in Newcastle.The brewery operates two taprooms and event spaces in Portland, along with the Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford—a year-round destination that combines wood-fired, farm-driven food with outdoor recreation. The Oxford campus features groomed ski and snowshoe trails in winter and an 18-hole disc golf course during warmer months, highlighting the brewery's commitment to community engagement and seasonal menus that showcase organic produce grown on-site.Oxbow Brewing Company's recent acquisition of Rising Tide Brewing Company has further solidified its standing in the Maine craft beer scene. This strategic partnership has enhanced operational efficiencies and expanded production capabilities while maintaining independent ownership of both brands. Warner's appointment is seen as a vital step in ensuring long-term stability and growth for Oxbow and its newly acquired partner, reinforcing the brewery's commitment to producing high-quality, balanced beers.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY PARTNERS WITH MICROSOFT IN AI POWER INITIATIVE

Constellation Energy is partnering with Microsoft to restart the Three Mile Island nuclear facility, aiming to supply power specifically for data centers. This strategic collaboration aligns with growing demands for carbon-free electricity in the rapidly expanding artificial intelligence sector. The agreement represents a significant shift in how energy is being sourced for digital infrastructure, with the potential for substantial implications in the clean energy landscape.The partnership with Microsoft, announced on 28th April 2026, positions Constellation Energy at the forefront of AI power sourcing discussions, especially as hyperscale data center operators increasingly seek reliable, carbon-free baseload power. This move not only enhances Constellation's existing generation portfolio but also adds a high-profile customer that reflects the energy demands of today’s tech-driven economy. The share price of Constellation Energy recently stood at $315.17, reflecting a notable increase of 40.9% over the past year.This deal dovetails with Constellation Energy's broader strategy of entering into large, contracted clean-power agreements. By tying a legacy nuclear asset to the burgeoning demand from AI-driven applications, the company is set to benefit from long-term visibility on volumes and pricing, particularly if the partnership is structured as a multi-year agreement. Analysts suggest that the implications of this agreement may alter the dynamics of nuclear energy discussions in the context of the clean energy transition.Investors should remain vigilant regarding the structure of the contracts associated with the Three Mile Island facility, including the duration and pricing mechanisms, as well as any commitments related to 24/7 carbon-free energy. The partnership could influence the competitive landscape, particularly as tech giants like Meta and other cloud providers weigh their options for similar agreements with energy suppliers. Moreover, the move comes at a time when Constellation Energy has seen a resurgence in stock price, despite a year-to-date decline of 13.9%.In the coming months, stakeholders will want to track updates on regulatory approvals and capital requirements for the Three Mile Island project. The successful execution of this plan, along with the integration of the recent Calpine acquisition and ongoing partnerships, will be crucial for Constellation Energy's growth trajectory. The company's ability to replicate these types of agreements with additional data center customers could further solidify its position in the clean energy market.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY FACES PRESSURE AS ANALYSTS ADJUST PRICE TARGETS

Shares of Constellation Energy (NASDAQ: CEG) are experiencing a notable decline today, driven by an analyst's recent downward adjustment of the stock's price target. As of 2:54 p.m. ET, Constellation Energy's stock is down 3.1%, continuing a downward trend that began after the company closed at $299.14 last Thursday. This decline follows the announcement made on 23rd April 2026 regarding the revised price forecast.Morgan Stanley, while maintaining a 'buy' rating on Constellation Energy, has lowered its price target to $360 from a previous $385. This adjustment has raised concerns among investors, although the new target still suggests a potential upside of approximately 25.2% from the stock's last closing price. The adjustment reflects a more cautious outlook but does not indicate a complete lack of confidence in the company's long-term prospects.Analysts have pointed out that while price targets can influence market sentiment, the fundamental performance of a company should be the primary focus for investors. Constellation Energy has been involved in nuclear energy, a sector that has seen fluctuating dynamics due to regulatory changes and the increasing demand for clean energy solutions. Investors are advised to weigh these factors alongside analyst projections when considering their positions in the stock.Despite the current volatility, some analysts remain optimistic about Constellation Energy's long-term growth potential. The company's commitment to expanding its renewable energy portfolio and reducing carbon emissions aligns with broader energy sector trends. This strategic direction could position Constellation Energy favorably as global energy policies increasingly favor sustainability.In conclusion, while the downward revision of the price target for Constellation Energy may cause short-term fluctuations in stock performance, investors are encouraged to consider the underlying fundamentals and industry trends. The current market reaction underscores the importance of a comprehensive analysis when making investment decisions in the energy sector.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION (CEG) DEFIES MARKET TRENDS WITH POSITIVE GAINS

Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) ended the recent trading session at $292.77, reflecting a +1.95% change from the preceding day's closing price. This upward movement outpaced the S&P 500's daily loss of 0.41%. On the same day, the Dow experienced a decline of 0.36%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq saw a more substantial drop of 0.89%.Despite this daily gain, shares of Constellation Energy Corporation have depreciated by 5.33% over the past month, trailing the Oils-Energy sector's loss of 0.95% and the S&P 500's strong gain of 9.71%. Market analysts and investors are now pivoting their attention to the upcoming earnings disclosure for Constellation Energy, scheduled for May 11, 2026, with expectations pointing towards an earnings report of $2.52 per share. This figure would signify a year-over-year growth of 17.76%, while revenue forecasts suggest $8.77 billion, marking a significant 29.22% increase compared to the same quarter last year.Looking ahead, full-year projections from the Zacks Consensus Estimates indicate earnings of $11.78 per share and revenue of $32.63 billion for Constellation Energy Corporation, reflecting changes of +25.45% and +27.81%, respectively, from the prior year. Investors are advised to monitor any recent adjustments to analyst forecasts, as these revisions often indicate shifts in near-term business trends. Upward revisions in estimates typically signal a positive outlook on Constellation Energy's operational capabilities and profitability.To navigate these changes effectively, investors can leverage the Zacks Rank, a proprietary model that incorporates estimate changes to provide actionable ratings. The Zacks Rank system, which ranges from #1 (Strong Buy) to #5 (Strong Sell), has demonstrated an impressive track record of outperformance, with #1 ranked stocks yielding an average annual return of +25% since 1988. Over the last 30 days, the consensus EPS projection for Constellation Energy Corporation has increased by 1.32%, and the company currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).From a valuation perspective, Constellation Energy Corporation is trading at a Forward P/E ratio of 24.38, higher than the industry average of 16.55, suggesting a premium valuation. Additionally, CEG's PEG ratio stands at 1.13, considerably lower than the industry average of 2.52. The Alternative Energy - Other industry, part of the Oils-Energy sector, holds a favorable Zacks Industry Rank of 72, placing it among the top 30% of over 250 industries, indicating potential for future growth. Investors are encouraged to utilize Zacks.com for tracking stock-moving metrics and other relevant data in the upcoming trading sessions.

+ NRG ENERGY [3 News That Matters]

+ NRG ENERGY SECURES $2.6 BILLION THROUGH STRATEGIC DEBT OFFERINGS

HOUSTON - NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) has successfully priced a substantial $2.6 billion in debt offerings across three distinct tranches, as announced in a recent statement. This strategic move comes on the heels of the decision made on 17th April 2026 to enhance the company's liquidity and refinance existing obligations.The offerings comprise $500 million in 4.955% senior secured first lien notes due 2031, alongside $1.05 billion in 5.875% senior unsecured notes due 2034 and another $1.05 billion in 6.125% senior unsecured notes due 2036. The new notes will be guaranteed by NRG Energy's current and future wholly-owned U.S. subsidiaries, providing additional security for investors. The secured notes are backed by a first priority security interest in collateral already pledged for NRG's existing credit agreements and senior secured notes.As of the end of the fourth quarter of 2025, NRG Energy's total debt stood at $16.6 billion. However, the company maintains a robust current ratio of 1.64, indicating that its liquid assets comfortably exceed short-term obligations. The net proceeds from the current debt offerings will be utilized to repay a portion of outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility and fund the tender price for a previously announced offer by its subsidiary Lightning Power, LLC for outstanding senior secured notes. Any residual proceeds may be allocated for general corporate purposes, including the repayment of other debts.The completion of each of the offerings is independent, with no conditions tied to the other offerings or the term loan B. The notes are being offered exclusively to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A and to non-U.S. persons outside the United States under Regulation S, with no registration under the Securities Act of 1933. This move highlights NRG Energy's commitment to optimizing its capital structure amidst a dynamic market environment.In related news, NRG Energy's subsidiary Lightning Power has initiated a tender offer to purchase up to $1.5 billion in senior secured notes due 2032, offering $1,063.75 per $1,000 principal amount for notes tendered by 27th April 2026, including an early tender payment. Analysts have responded positively to NRG's strategic maneuvers, with Jefferies raising its price target to $199 and Wolfe Research upgrading the stock to Outperform, both citing the anticipated growth in the sector. Goldman Sachs has also reinstated coverage with a Buy rating, emphasizing the positive impact of NRG's recent acquisitions, which are expected to bolster its business profile moving forward.

+ NRG ENERGY UNVEILS NEW OFFERINGS OF SECURED AND UNSECURED NOTES

NRG Energy, Inc. has announced the commencement of concurrent offerings of senior secured notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036. On 14th April 2026, the company revealed that these notes will be guaranteed by its U.S. subsidiaries, with the secured notes backed by a first-priority lien on substantially all assets pledged under its existing credit facilities.The proceeds from these offerings are intended to be utilized in conjunction with a proposed $900 million term loan B, aimed primarily at repaying borrowings under NRG Energy's revolving credit facility. Additionally, the funds will support a tender offer for Lightning Power’s 7.25% senior secured notes, which are due in 2032. NRG Energy emphasizes that any remaining proceeds will be allocated towards paying fees and expenses, as well as general corporate purposes, including potential debt repayment.The announcement has been positively received in the market, as evidenced by a 3.76% increase in NRG Energy's shares following the news. Investors are keenly watching the company's financial maneuvers, particularly as it seeks to optimize its capital structure and enhance liquidity through these note offerings.This strategic move comes at a time when NRG Energy is focusing on strengthening its balance sheet and positioning itself for future growth opportunities. The successful execution of these offerings could provide the company with the necessary resources to manage its debt effectively while pursuing its operational goals.As NRG Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy landscape, the market will be attentive to how these financial strategies play out. Investors are encouraged to monitor the company's developments closely, as the implications of this notes offering could significantly impact its long-term financial health.

+ NRG ENERGY TARGETS DEBT MARKETS WITH NEW OFFERINGS AND $900 MILLION LOAN

NRG Energy, Inc. is making significant strides in the debt markets with a multi-tranche offering aimed at refinancing existing obligations and extending maturities. On 14th April 2026, NRG Energy announced the launch of concurrent offerings of senior secured first-lien notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036.The proceeds from these offerings, along with a new $900 million term loan B, will primarily be utilized to repay borrowings under NRG’s revolving credit facility and to fund a tender offer for outstanding 7.25% senior secured notes due 2032, which were issued by its subsidiary Lightning Power LLC. Additional funds may be allocated for general corporate purposes, including further debt reduction.The secured notes will be backed by a first-priority lien on a substantial portion of NRG's assets, mirroring collateral pledged under its existing credit facilities and secured notes. In contrast, the unsecured notes will rank behind the secured debt, offering investors a higher yield in exchange for increased risk — a choice that reflects the current appetite for yield among fixed-income investors.This transaction is indicative of a broader refinancing trend among U.S. independent power producers, many of whom are seeking to term out near-dated liabilities and lower funding costs following a period of elevated interest rates. The energy sector is undergoing a transformation as companies like NRG Energy position themselves for a surge in electricity demand driven by burgeoning data centers and electrification initiatives.As NRG Energy continues to navigate these dynamic market conditions, its latest debt offerings could attract fresh investor interest, particularly as power infrastructure becomes increasingly critical in the age of artificial intelligence. The steps taken by NRG demonstrate a proactive approach in optimizing its capital structure and extending financial flexibility.

DASHBOARD: OXBOW

PermID4296883232Websitehttps://www.oxbow.com/IndustryOil and Gas

ACTIVITIES:

Oxbow Corporation, founded in 1983 by William I. Koch in West Palm Beach, Florida, is a privately held energy and commodities company. Specializing in petroleum coke, coal, and carbon products, Oxbow generates over $4 billion annually, operating in 40 countries. With 1,200 employees, its subsidiaries, like Oxbow Carbon, focus on calcined coke for aluminum and steel industries. Oxbows logistics network ensures efficient global distribution. The company invests in renewable energy and carbon management, aligning with sustainability trends. Despite legal disputes, including a $1 billion tax settlement in 2021, Oxbow remains a key player in energy markets, leveraging innovation and strategic partnerships to drive growth.

+ OXBOW [3 News That Matters]

+ OXBOW BREWING'S LEADERSHIP SHIFT: A NEW ERA UNDER BRI WARNER

Oxbow Brewing Company is undergoing a significant leadership transition as Bri Warner, the new president, assumes a more active role in the brewery's day-to-day operations. As of 18th March 2026, Warner is just two months into her position, allowing founder Tim Adams to concentrate on promoting Oxbow and its recently acquired brand, Rising Tide.Warner, who previously worked at Atlantic Sea Farms, the largest kelp farming operation in the United States, brings a fresh perspective to Oxbow Brewing. In a recent podcast recorded at Oxbow’s Portland taproom, Adams expressed his confidence in Warner’s capabilities, stating, "Bri just is an absolute master at doing that." This transition is seen as essential for the company's long-term sustainability, particularly as it manages multiple brands and locations.With Warner at the forefront of operations, Adams has been able to step back from daily management and focus on brand representation. This winter, he has spent considerable time at Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford, Maine, engaging with trade partners and consumers while overseeing the brewery's various offerings in a picturesque setting. Warner noted, "What that does is it keeps Tim from being out on the road, and I think probably the most power of this is the fact that we get to free him to do the work to continue to grow the brand in a really great and passionate way."The leadership change comes at a time when many breweries face challenges in balancing operational demands with brand growth. Warner aims to bridge this gap at Oxbow Brewing, leveraging her expertise to tighten internal operations while maintaining the brewery's focus on quality and creativity. She commented, "I think that you see a lot of breweries and a lot of people in the industry that are having a hard time bridging that gap, and hopefully this move will allow that to do that."As Oxbow Brewing navigates this new chapter, the industry is watching closely. The podcast also covered other recent developments in the beer sector, including Tilray’s acquisition of BrewDog’s Australian operations and the launch of George Clooney’s new non-alcoholic brand. With strategic leadership in place, Oxbow Brewing is poised for continued growth and innovation.

+ OXBOW RESORT EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH BERKSHIRE PIZZERIA ACQUISITION

CHARLEMONT — Oxbow Resort owner Travis DeCere has expanded his business interests by acquiring Berkshire Pizzeria and the former Otters restaurant, marking a significant investment in the local dining scene. DeCere, who has been renovating the Oxbow Resort Motel for over a year, solidified his plans on 8th February 2026, finalizing the purchase of the pizzeria for $330,000 through Berkshire Equity LLC.During his frequent trips to Charlemont for the Oxbow renovations, DeCere connected with Greg Rowehl, the previous owner of Berkshire Pizzeria. Initially focused on the motel, DeCere found himself intrigued by the restaurant opportunity. Now, with the motel nearing completion and extended-stay tenants occupying the back buildings, he is ready to embrace this new challenge alongside his business partner, Colleen Brady.Brady, who has relocated to Charlemont from Florida, brings substantial culinary experience, having owned food trucks in the past. She plans to introduce several changes to the pizzeria, including an expanded menu featuring fresh chicken tenders and potential pasta options. The duo aims to enhance the restaurant's ambiance and offerings, including a full bar on the back deck, in hopes of attracting more customers, especially skiers from Berkshire East Mountain Resort.In addition to the pizzeria, DeCere also acquired the former Otters restaurant property for $250,000 and plans to find a tenant to create a full-service restaurant. The Otters property, located at 1745 Route 2, provides ample space with a full kitchen and bar, and is currently listed for lease on commercial real estate platforms. This strategic acquisition represents a commitment to enriching the culinary landscape of Charlemont while building upon the legacy established by the previous owners of the pizzeria.As Oxbow Resort continues to evolve, DeCere and Brady are focused on honoring the legacy of the Rowehl family while also injecting their own vision into the local dining experience. With plans to refresh the pizzeria's interior and expand its operational hours, they aim to create a community hub that reflects the charm of Charlemont.

+ OXBOW BREWING COMPANY APPOINTS BRIANA WARNER AS NEW PRESIDENT

PORTLAND, MAINE - Oxbow Brewing Company has announced the appointment of Briana Warner as President, marking a significant leadership change for the brewery. Since its beginnings in Newcastle, Maine, Oxbow has grown with patience and purpose, guided by a deep respect for craft, community, and place. The announcement was made on the 11th of February 2026, as the company looks toward its next chapter under Warner's leadership.Warner, who previously served as CEO of Atlantic Sea Farms and has an extensive background as a U.S. diplomat, brings a wealth of experience in scaling mission-driven businesses while keeping community values at the forefront. Her tenure at Atlantic Sea Farms saw the company evolve into the largest farmed kelp operation in the United States, establishing a nationally recognized brand rooted in Maine's working waterfront. As President of Oxbow Brewing Company, she will be responsible for leading company strategy, overseeing daily operations, and guiding the brewery's growth across its wholesale and hospitality sectors.Tim Adams, the Founder of Oxbow Brewing Company, will continue to play an active role within the company, focusing on brand development, creative direction, sales, and community engagement. 'Briana is an outstanding leader and a natural fit for Oxbow,' said Adams. 'Her experience and values align perfectly with our commitment to thoughtful, sustainable growth.' This leadership evolution comes at a pivotal time for Oxbow, which has seen its distribution extend well beyond its original farmhouse brewery in Newcastle.The brewery operates two taprooms and event spaces in Portland, along with the Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford—a year-round destination that combines wood-fired, farm-driven food with outdoor recreation. The Oxford campus features groomed ski and snowshoe trails in winter and an 18-hole disc golf course during warmer months, highlighting the brewery's commitment to community engagement and seasonal menus that showcase organic produce grown on-site.Oxbow Brewing Company's recent acquisition of Rising Tide Brewing Company has further solidified its standing in the Maine craft beer scene. This strategic partnership has enhanced operational efficiencies and expanded production capabilities while maintaining independent ownership of both brands. Warner's appointment is seen as a vital step in ensuring long-term stability and growth for Oxbow and its newly acquired partner, reinforcing the brewery's commitment to producing high-quality, balanced beers.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY PARTNERS WITH MICROSOFT IN AI POWER INITIATIVE

Constellation Energy is partnering with Microsoft to restart the Three Mile Island nuclear facility, aiming to supply power specifically for data centers. This strategic collaboration aligns with growing demands for carbon-free electricity in the rapidly expanding artificial intelligence sector. The agreement represents a significant shift in how energy is being sourced for digital infrastructure, with the potential for substantial implications in the clean energy landscape.The partnership with Microsoft, announced on 28th April 2026, positions Constellation Energy at the forefront of AI power sourcing discussions, especially as hyperscale data center operators increasingly seek reliable, carbon-free baseload power. This move not only enhances Constellation's existing generation portfolio but also adds a high-profile customer that reflects the energy demands of today’s tech-driven economy. The share price of Constellation Energy recently stood at $315.17, reflecting a notable increase of 40.9% over the past year.This deal dovetails with Constellation Energy's broader strategy of entering into large, contracted clean-power agreements. By tying a legacy nuclear asset to the burgeoning demand from AI-driven applications, the company is set to benefit from long-term visibility on volumes and pricing, particularly if the partnership is structured as a multi-year agreement. Analysts suggest that the implications of this agreement may alter the dynamics of nuclear energy discussions in the context of the clean energy transition.Investors should remain vigilant regarding the structure of the contracts associated with the Three Mile Island facility, including the duration and pricing mechanisms, as well as any commitments related to 24/7 carbon-free energy. The partnership could influence the competitive landscape, particularly as tech giants like Meta and other cloud providers weigh their options for similar agreements with energy suppliers. Moreover, the move comes at a time when Constellation Energy has seen a resurgence in stock price, despite a year-to-date decline of 13.9%.In the coming months, stakeholders will want to track updates on regulatory approvals and capital requirements for the Three Mile Island project. The successful execution of this plan, along with the integration of the recent Calpine acquisition and ongoing partnerships, will be crucial for Constellation Energy's growth trajectory. The company's ability to replicate these types of agreements with additional data center customers could further solidify its position in the clean energy market.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY FACES PRESSURE AS ANALYSTS ADJUST PRICE TARGETS

Shares of Constellation Energy (NASDAQ: CEG) are experiencing a notable decline today, driven by an analyst's recent downward adjustment of the stock's price target. As of 2:54 p.m. ET, Constellation Energy's stock is down 3.1%, continuing a downward trend that began after the company closed at $299.14 last Thursday. This decline follows the announcement made on 23rd April 2026 regarding the revised price forecast.Morgan Stanley, while maintaining a 'buy' rating on Constellation Energy, has lowered its price target to $360 from a previous $385. This adjustment has raised concerns among investors, although the new target still suggests a potential upside of approximately 25.2% from the stock's last closing price. The adjustment reflects a more cautious outlook but does not indicate a complete lack of confidence in the company's long-term prospects.Analysts have pointed out that while price targets can influence market sentiment, the fundamental performance of a company should be the primary focus for investors. Constellation Energy has been involved in nuclear energy, a sector that has seen fluctuating dynamics due to regulatory changes and the increasing demand for clean energy solutions. Investors are advised to weigh these factors alongside analyst projections when considering their positions in the stock.Despite the current volatility, some analysts remain optimistic about Constellation Energy's long-term growth potential. The company's commitment to expanding its renewable energy portfolio and reducing carbon emissions aligns with broader energy sector trends. This strategic direction could position Constellation Energy favorably as global energy policies increasingly favor sustainability.In conclusion, while the downward revision of the price target for Constellation Energy may cause short-term fluctuations in stock performance, investors are encouraged to consider the underlying fundamentals and industry trends. The current market reaction underscores the importance of a comprehensive analysis when making investment decisions in the energy sector.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION (CEG) DEFIES MARKET TRENDS WITH POSITIVE GAINS

Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) ended the recent trading session at $292.77, reflecting a +1.95% change from the preceding day's closing price. This upward movement outpaced the S&P 500's daily loss of 0.41%. On the same day, the Dow experienced a decline of 0.36%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq saw a more substantial drop of 0.89%.Despite this daily gain, shares of Constellation Energy Corporation have depreciated by 5.33% over the past month, trailing the Oils-Energy sector's loss of 0.95% and the S&P 500's strong gain of 9.71%. Market analysts and investors are now pivoting their attention to the upcoming earnings disclosure for Constellation Energy, scheduled for May 11, 2026, with expectations pointing towards an earnings report of $2.52 per share. This figure would signify a year-over-year growth of 17.76%, while revenue forecasts suggest $8.77 billion, marking a significant 29.22% increase compared to the same quarter last year.Looking ahead, full-year projections from the Zacks Consensus Estimates indicate earnings of $11.78 per share and revenue of $32.63 billion for Constellation Energy Corporation, reflecting changes of +25.45% and +27.81%, respectively, from the prior year. Investors are advised to monitor any recent adjustments to analyst forecasts, as these revisions often indicate shifts in near-term business trends. Upward revisions in estimates typically signal a positive outlook on Constellation Energy's operational capabilities and profitability.To navigate these changes effectively, investors can leverage the Zacks Rank, a proprietary model that incorporates estimate changes to provide actionable ratings. The Zacks Rank system, which ranges from #1 (Strong Buy) to #5 (Strong Sell), has demonstrated an impressive track record of outperformance, with #1 ranked stocks yielding an average annual return of +25% since 1988. Over the last 30 days, the consensus EPS projection for Constellation Energy Corporation has increased by 1.32%, and the company currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).From a valuation perspective, Constellation Energy Corporation is trading at a Forward P/E ratio of 24.38, higher than the industry average of 16.55, suggesting a premium valuation. Additionally, CEG's PEG ratio stands at 1.13, considerably lower than the industry average of 2.52. The Alternative Energy - Other industry, part of the Oils-Energy sector, holds a favorable Zacks Industry Rank of 72, placing it among the top 30% of over 250 industries, indicating potential for future growth. Investors are encouraged to utilize Zacks.com for tracking stock-moving metrics and other relevant data in the upcoming trading sessions.
