value.Title: Waste Management Q1 2026 Earnings Snapshot and Strategic Priorities *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-8C73-RSVS-J4KJ-00000-00Waste Management Q1 2026 Earnings Snapshot and Strategic Priorities *Updated*2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:43ZLexisNexisWaste Management Q1 2026 Earnings Snapshot and Strategic Priorities *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

This article includes relevant portions of a research report that we wrote and published analyzing Waste Management's Q1 2026 10-Q filing, which was made available following the company's earnings release on April 28, 2026.

INDEX

SECTION 1 Quarter Ending March 31, 2026 Strategic Priorities Summary

SECTION 2 Financials as reported (Quarter ending 31 March 2026)

SECTION 1 Quarter Ending March 31, 2026 Strategic Priorities Summary

Growth Objectives

From a top-line expansion perspective, the company successfully executed its revenue growth targets through a highly disciplined pricing strategy, achieving a core price metric of 6.3 percent for the quarter. This robust pricing power was further complemented by a collection and disposal yield of 3.9 percent. Together, these pricing and yield metrics served as the primary catalysts for the overall revenue growth of 3.5 percent reported in the quarter, underscoring the inelasticity of the core service model and management's capability to outpace inflationary cost pressures through targeted pricing actions.

A central priority within the growth framework is the continuous optimization of the overarching business mix. Strategic emphasis remains heavily skewed toward capturing and retaining higher-margin municipal solid waste (MSW) volumes, which provide a stable, recurring revenue baseline with favorable profitability characteristics. Beyond the traditional solid waste operations, the company is realizing continued volume growth within its Recycling and Renewable Energy segments, directly attributable to the commercialization and operational scaling of recently completed sustainability growth projects.

Specifically, WM successfully completed the commissioning and operational ramp-up of three major recycling facilities and automation projects situated in key strategic geographic markets: Ontario, Detroit, and South Florida. The activation of these specialized assets injected nearly 300,000 tons of advanced processing capacity into the company's infrastructure network. The strategic deployment of this capacity in growing, high-demand markets effectively drove higher aggregate recycling volumes and facilitated increased processing throughput. Parallel to these recycling advancements, these capital projects structurally augmented the company's renewable natural gas production capabilities, establishing a robust foundation for future environmental commodity monetization.

Within the Healthcare Solutions vertical, the strategic growth mandate is highly focused on post-integration synergy capture and rigorous operational integration. Through effective SG&A cost management protocols and streamlined service delivery models, the medical waste platform delivered substantial profitability improvements. This disciplined approach yielded an operating EBITDA growth of 18.4 percent, which normalizes to an 11.6 percent growth rate on an adjusted basis. The ability to extract double-digit adjusted EBITDA growth from this specialized segment validates the strategic rationale of the medical waste platform expansion. Based on the aggregate performance across these diverse growth vectors and the realization of targeted operational metrics in the first quarter, the company reaffirms its full-year 2026 financial outlook, signaling deep institutional confidence in the execution of its operational plan for the balance of the fiscal year.

Innovation Objectives

WM continues to prioritize substantial investments in technology and automation as primary levers to structurally optimize its fixed and variable cost structure, enhance baseline operational efficiency, and provide critical support for frontline workforce retention in a competitive labor market. The strategic deployment of automation projects, particularly within the recycling operations segment, has proven to be highly accretive. By reducing manual sorting dependencies and increasing optical and robotic processing capabilities, these automation investments directly contributed to the improved processing volumes and expanded operating margins observed during the quarter.

Furthermore, the infusion of technology and automation across the broader Collection and Disposal business segments has been instrumental in safeguarding profitability metrics. By optimizing routing density, improving fleet utilization, and reducing localized operational friction, these technological investments helped deliver and sustain favorable price-to-cost spreads throughout the quarter. Management views these technological integrations not merely as cost-mitigation tools, but as structural enhancements that elevate the baseline margin profile of the legacy business.

In tandem with recycling and collection automation, the company continues to aggressively advance its renewable energy platform. This is achieved through the disciplined funding and execution of targeted growth projects designed to materially increase total renewable natural gas output. By scaling production capacity, the company is structurally positioned to benefit from the favorable pricing dynamics of higher natural gas and electricity prices in the broader energy markets. These targeted initiatives represent integral components of the broader, long-term strategic investment thesis encompassing recycling, renewable energy, medical waste, technology, and fleet modernization. The ultimate financial objective of these innovation-driven capital deployments is the harvesting of long-term economic benefits and the acceleration of robust free cash flow profiles. The efficacy of this capital allocation strategy was explicitly demonstrated during the quarter, as free cash flow nearly doubled year-over-year to reach a record $920 million.

Competitive Positioning

WM's strategic and capital initiatives continually reinforce its entrenched positioning as North America's leading provider of comprehensive environmental solutions. This market leadership is physically supported by an unmatched, highly integrated asset network that includes the industry's largest disposal network, a vastly scaled collection fleet, and the most expansive recycling operations on the continent. Furthermore, the company maintains a dominant position in the beneficial use of landfill gas, supported by an extensive portfolio of renewable natural gas plants, landfill gas-to-electricity plants, and the industry's premier heavy-duty natural gas truck fleet. This unparalleled infrastructure scale establishes formidable barriers to entry and provides structural cost advantages over regional peers.

The financial results of the quarter highlight the fundamental resilience of the company's integrated business model. The internalization of waste streams and the localized density of the collection network enabled the core Collection and Disposal business to generate an operating EBITDA growth of $154 million on a reported basis. Importantly, this absolute dollar growth was accompanied by a significant margin expansion of 190 basis points, a metric achieved despite notable weather-related volume impacts and operational disruptions during the period.

The successful translation of top-line pricing into bottom-line profitability is further evidenced by the improvement in the company's operating expense profile. Through relentless cost optimization protocols and the aforementioned automation initiatives, operating expenses as a percentage of revenue improved by 70 basis points, declining to 59.3 percent (and 59.2 percent on an adjusted basis). This reduction in operating expense intensity demonstrates management's tight control over unit costs and the successful structural shift toward a more technologically enabled, lower-cost operating model.

Underpinning the company's operational execution is a framework of strict financial discipline, which was clearly demonstrated by the substantial return of capital to shareholders during the first quarter. The company executed $729 million in total shareholder returns, composed of $385 million distributed through regular dividend payments and an additional $344 million deployed through strategic share repurchases. Crucially, this aggressive return of capital was executed while strictly maintaining the corporate leverage ratio well within the board-approved target range of 2.5 to 3.0 times total debt to EBITDA.

The company emphasizes that its flexible, investment-grade balance sheet remains a distinct competitive advantage, enabling it to aggressively fund organic growth projects, pursue accretive M&A opportunities, and continuously return capital to shareholders while retaining the capacity to capitalize on emerging opportunities in any economic environment. Forward-looking statements consistently reference management's ongoing, localized focus on organic volume growth, sustained earnings expansion, long-term shareholder value creation, the delivery of stated performance results, the realization of financial targets, the adherence to provided financial guidance, the continued deployment of technology and automation investments, and the precise overall execution of the 2026 financial outlook.

urn:contentItem:6JG9-MBF3-S1JR-B4FH-00000-00Secures New Rise Ground Lease Forbearance Deal2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:42ZLexisNexisSecures New Rise Ground Lease Forbearance Deal

XCF GLOBAL INC ("SAFX-Q") - Secures New Rise Ground Lease Forbearance Deal

Agreement continues through January 1, 2027, subject to specified conditions, supporting New Rise's planned upgrade path and operational progress.

XCF Global, Inc. ("XCF"), an emerging player in lowering emissions and strengthening domestic renewable energy resilience of the aviation industry through Sustainable Aviation Fuel ("SAF"), announced that New Rise Renewables Reno, LLC ("New Rise"), a subsidiary of XCF, has entered into a forbearance agreement dated April 27, 2026 (the "Agreement") with Twain GL XXVIII, LLC (the "Landlord") in connection with the ground lease for New Rise's Reno, Nevada facility.

New Rise Reno was commissioned in February 2025 and has produced SAF, renewable diesel, and renewable naphtha. Since the start of commercial operations in March 2025, the facility has produced more than 2.5 million gallons of renewable fuels. New Rise Reno is in the final stages of its planned upgrade, intended to strengthen long-term operability and repeatability. Current workstreams are focused on improving operating stability and equipment readiness, reinforcing quality systems required for certified fuel, and strengthening start-up and operating procedures. XCF is targeting a return to operations in June, subject to completion of upgrade activities and standard start-up procedures.

Under the Agreement, the Landlord agreed to forbear from exercising certain rights and remedies under the applicable lease documents with respect to certain alleged events of default through January 1, 2027, subject to New Rise's compliance with the terms and conditions of the Agreement including the required payments therein. For more information, please see the form of agreement filed today on Form 8-k.

About XCF Global, Inc.

XCF Global, Inc. ("XCF") is an emerging sustainable aviation fuel company dedicated to accelerating the aviation industry's transition to net-zero emissions. Our flagship facility, New Rise Renewables Reno, has a permitted nameplate production capacity of 38 million gallons per year, positioning XCF as an early mover among large-scale SAF producers in North America. XCF is working to advance a pipeline of potential expansion opportunities in Nevada, North Carolina, and Florida, and to build partnerships across the energy and transportation sectors to scale SAF globally. XCF is listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market and trades under the ticker, SAFX.

To learn more, visit www.xcf.global

Contacts

XCF Global: Corporate Comms

media@xcf.global

All statements, other than statements of historical facts, are forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements concern future circumstances and results and other statements that are not historical facts and are sometimes identified by the words "aim," "may," "will," "should," "potential," "intend," "expect," "endeavor," "seek," "anticipate," "estimate," "overestimate," "underestimate," "believe," "plan," "could," "would," "project," "predict," "continue," "target," "objective," "goal," "designed," or the negatives of these words or other similar terms or expressions that concern XCF's expectations, strategy, priorities, plans, or intentions. Should one or more of these risks or uncertainties materialize, or should underlying assumptions prove incorrect, actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements.

We can give no assurance that such plans, estimates, or expectations will be achieved, and therefore, actual results may differ materially from any plans, estimates, or expectations in such forward-looking statements.

Forward-looking statements are based on current expectations, estimates, assumptions and projections and involve known and unknown risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results, developments or outcomes to differ materially from those expressed or implied by such statements. Important factors that could cause actual results, developments or outcomes to differ materially include, among others: (1) changes in domestic and foreign business, market, financial, political, and legal conditions; (2) unexpected increases in XCF Global's expenses, including manufacturing and operating expenses and interest expenses, as a result of potential inflationary pressures, changes in interest rates and other factors; (3) the occurrence of any event, change or other circumstances that could give rise to the termination of negotiations and any agreements with regard to XCF Global's business combination agreement with DevvStream Corp. and Southern Energy Renewables Inc. (the "Business Combination") and/or its offtake arrangements; (4) the outcome of any legal proceedings that may be instituted against the parties to the Business Combination or others; (5) XCF Global's ability to regain compliance with Nasdaq's continued listing standards and thereafter continue to meet Nasdaq's continued listing standards; (6) XCF Global's ability to integrate the operations of New Rise and implement its business plan on its anticipated timeline; (7) XCF Global's ability to raise financing to fund its operations and business plan and the terms of any such financing; (8) the New Rise Reno production facility's ability to produce the anticipated quantities of SAF without interruption or material changes to the SAF production process; (9) the New Rise Reno production facility's ability to produce renewable diesel in commercial quantities without interruption during the ongoing SAF ramp-up process; (10) XCF Global's ability to resolve current disputes between its New Rise subsidiary and its landlord with respect to the ground lease for the New Rise Reno facility; (11) XCF Global's ability to resolve current disputes between its New Rise subsidiary and its primary lender with respect to loans outstanding that were used in the development of the New Rise Reno facility; (12) payment of fees, expenses and other costs related to the completion of the Business Combination and the New Rise acquisitions; (13) the risk of disruption to the current plans and operations of XCF Global as a result of the consummation of the Business Combination; (14) XCF Global's ability to recognize the anticipated benefits of the Business Combination and the New Rise acquisitions, which may be affected by, among other things, competition, the ability of XCF Global to grow and manage growth profitably, maintain relationships with customers and suppliers and retain its management and key employees; (15) changes in applicable laws or regulations; (16) risks related to extensive regulation, compliance obligations and rigorous enforcement by federal, state, and non-U.S. governmental authorities; (17) the possibility that XCF Global may be adversely affected by other economic, business, and/or competitive factors; (18) the availability of tax credits and other federal, state or local government support; (19) risks relating to XCF Global's and New Rise's key intellectual property rights, including the possible infringement of their intellectual property rights by third parties; (20) the risk that XCF Global's reporting and compliance obligations as a publicly-traded company divert management resources from business operations (21) LOIs and MOUs may not advance to definitive agreements or commercial deployment; (22) the effects of increased costs associated with operating as a public company and (23) various factors beyond management's control, including general economic conditions and other risks, uncertainties and factors set forth in XCF Global's filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), including its most recent Form 10-K, filed with the SEC on March 31, 2026, this Press Release and other filings XCF Global made or will make with the SEC in the future. In addition, forward-looking statements reflect XCF Global's expectations, plans or forecasts of future events and views as of the date of this Press Release. urn:contentItem:6JG8-YRR3-RTSP-Y2B4-00000-00OXBOW - RECENT NEWS OF CONSTELLATION ENERGY AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:43ZLexisNexisSTOCKOXBOW - RECENT NEWS OF CONSTELLATION ENERGY AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: OXBOW

PermID4296883232Websitehttps://www.oxbow.com/IndustryOil and Gas

ACTIVITIES:

Oxbow Corporation, founded in 1983 by William I. Koch in West Palm Beach, Florida, is a privately held energy and commodities company. Specializing in petroleum coke, coal, and carbon products, Oxbow generates over $4 billion annually, operating in 40 countries. With 1,200 employees, its subsidiaries, like Oxbow Carbon, focus on calcined coke for aluminum and steel industries. Oxbows logistics network ensures efficient global distribution. The company invests in renewable energy and carbon management, aligning with sustainability trends. Despite legal disputes, including a $1 billion tax settlement in 2021, Oxbow remains a key player in energy markets, leveraging innovation and strategic partnerships to drive growth.

+ OXBOW [3 News That Matters]

+ OXBOW BREWING'S LEADERSHIP SHIFT: A NEW ERA UNDER BRI WARNER

Oxbow Brewing Company is undergoing a significant leadership transition as Bri Warner, the new president, assumes a more active role in the brewery's day-to-day operations. As of 18th March 2026, Warner is just two months into her position, allowing founder Tim Adams to concentrate on promoting Oxbow and its recently acquired brand, Rising Tide.Warner, who previously worked at Atlantic Sea Farms, the largest kelp farming operation in the United States, brings a fresh perspective to Oxbow Brewing. In a recent podcast recorded at Oxbow’s Portland taproom, Adams expressed his confidence in Warner’s capabilities, stating, "Bri just is an absolute master at doing that." This transition is seen as essential for the company's long-term sustainability, particularly as it manages multiple brands and locations.With Warner at the forefront of operations, Adams has been able to step back from daily management and focus on brand representation. This winter, he has spent considerable time at Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford, Maine, engaging with trade partners and consumers while overseeing the brewery's various offerings in a picturesque setting. Warner noted, "What that does is it keeps Tim from being out on the road, and I think probably the most power of this is the fact that we get to free him to do the work to continue to grow the brand in a really great and passionate way."The leadership change comes at a time when many breweries face challenges in balancing operational demands with brand growth. Warner aims to bridge this gap at Oxbow Brewing, leveraging her expertise to tighten internal operations while maintaining the brewery's focus on quality and creativity. She commented, "I think that you see a lot of breweries and a lot of people in the industry that are having a hard time bridging that gap, and hopefully this move will allow that to do that."As Oxbow Brewing navigates this new chapter, the industry is watching closely. The podcast also covered other recent developments in the beer sector, including Tilray’s acquisition of BrewDog’s Australian operations and the launch of George Clooney’s new non-alcoholic brand. With strategic leadership in place, Oxbow Brewing is poised for continued growth and innovation.

+ OXBOW RESORT EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH BERKSHIRE PIZZERIA ACQUISITION

CHARLEMONT — Oxbow Resort owner Travis DeCere has expanded his business interests by acquiring Berkshire Pizzeria and the former Otters restaurant, marking a significant investment in the local dining scene. DeCere, who has been renovating the Oxbow Resort Motel for over a year, solidified his plans on 8th February 2026, finalizing the purchase of the pizzeria for $330,000 through Berkshire Equity LLC.During his frequent trips to Charlemont for the Oxbow renovations, DeCere connected with Greg Rowehl, the previous owner of Berkshire Pizzeria. Initially focused on the motel, DeCere found himself intrigued by the restaurant opportunity. Now, with the motel nearing completion and extended-stay tenants occupying the back buildings, he is ready to embrace this new challenge alongside his business partner, Colleen Brady.Brady, who has relocated to Charlemont from Florida, brings substantial culinary experience, having owned food trucks in the past. She plans to introduce several changes to the pizzeria, including an expanded menu featuring fresh chicken tenders and potential pasta options. The duo aims to enhance the restaurant's ambiance and offerings, including a full bar on the back deck, in hopes of attracting more customers, especially skiers from Berkshire East Mountain Resort.In addition to the pizzeria, DeCere also acquired the former Otters restaurant property for $250,000 and plans to find a tenant to create a full-service restaurant. The Otters property, located at 1745 Route 2, provides ample space with a full kitchen and bar, and is currently listed for lease on commercial real estate platforms. This strategic acquisition represents a commitment to enriching the culinary landscape of Charlemont while building upon the legacy established by the previous owners of the pizzeria.As Oxbow Resort continues to evolve, DeCere and Brady are focused on honoring the legacy of the Rowehl family while also injecting their own vision into the local dining experience. With plans to refresh the pizzeria's interior and expand its operational hours, they aim to create a community hub that reflects the charm of Charlemont.

+ OXBOW BREWING COMPANY APPOINTS BRIANA WARNER AS NEW PRESIDENT

PORTLAND, MAINE - Oxbow Brewing Company has announced the appointment of Briana Warner as President, marking a significant leadership change for the brewery. Since its beginnings in Newcastle, Maine, Oxbow has grown with patience and purpose, guided by a deep respect for craft, community, and place. The announcement was made on the 11th of February 2026, as the company looks toward its next chapter under Warner's leadership.Warner, who previously served as CEO of Atlantic Sea Farms and has an extensive background as a U.S. diplomat, brings a wealth of experience in scaling mission-driven businesses while keeping community values at the forefront. Her tenure at Atlantic Sea Farms saw the company evolve into the largest farmed kelp operation in the United States, establishing a nationally recognized brand rooted in Maine's working waterfront. As President of Oxbow Brewing Company, she will be responsible for leading company strategy, overseeing daily operations, and guiding the brewery's growth across its wholesale and hospitality sectors.Tim Adams, the Founder of Oxbow Brewing Company, will continue to play an active role within the company, focusing on brand development, creative direction, sales, and community engagement. 'Briana is an outstanding leader and a natural fit for Oxbow,' said Adams. 'Her experience and values align perfectly with our commitment to thoughtful, sustainable growth.' This leadership evolution comes at a pivotal time for Oxbow, which has seen its distribution extend well beyond its original farmhouse brewery in Newcastle.The brewery operates two taprooms and event spaces in Portland, along with the Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford—a year-round destination that combines wood-fired, farm-driven food with outdoor recreation. The Oxford campus features groomed ski and snowshoe trails in winter and an 18-hole disc golf course during warmer months, highlighting the brewery's commitment to community engagement and seasonal menus that showcase organic produce grown on-site.Oxbow Brewing Company's recent acquisition of Rising Tide Brewing Company has further solidified its standing in the Maine craft beer scene. This strategic partnership has enhanced operational efficiencies and expanded production capabilities while maintaining independent ownership of both brands. Warner's appointment is seen as a vital step in ensuring long-term stability and growth for Oxbow and its newly acquired partner, reinforcing the brewery's commitment to producing high-quality, balanced beers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY PARTNERS WITH MICROSOFT IN AI POWER INITIATIVE

Constellation Energy is partnering with Microsoft to restart the Three Mile Island nuclear facility, aiming to supply power specifically for data centers. This strategic collaboration aligns with growing demands for carbon-free electricity in the rapidly expanding artificial intelligence sector. The agreement represents a significant shift in how energy is being sourced for digital infrastructure, with the potential for substantial implications in the clean energy landscape.The partnership with Microsoft, announced on 28th April 2026, positions Constellation Energy at the forefront of AI power sourcing discussions, especially as hyperscale data center operators increasingly seek reliable, carbon-free baseload power. This move not only enhances Constellation's existing generation portfolio but also adds a high-profile customer that reflects the energy demands of today’s tech-driven economy. The share price of Constellation Energy recently stood at $315.17, reflecting a notable increase of 40.9% over the past year.This deal dovetails with Constellation Energy's broader strategy of entering into large, contracted clean-power agreements. By tying a legacy nuclear asset to the burgeoning demand from AI-driven applications, the company is set to benefit from long-term visibility on volumes and pricing, particularly if the partnership is structured as a multi-year agreement. Analysts suggest that the implications of this agreement may alter the dynamics of nuclear energy discussions in the context of the clean energy transition.Investors should remain vigilant regarding the structure of the contracts associated with the Three Mile Island facility, including the duration and pricing mechanisms, as well as any commitments related to 24/7 carbon-free energy. The partnership could influence the competitive landscape, particularly as tech giants like Meta and other cloud providers weigh their options for similar agreements with energy suppliers. Moreover, the move comes at a time when Constellation Energy has seen a resurgence in stock price, despite a year-to-date decline of 13.9%.In the coming months, stakeholders will want to track updates on regulatory approvals and capital requirements for the Three Mile Island project. The successful execution of this plan, along with the integration of the recent Calpine acquisition and ongoing partnerships, will be crucial for Constellation Energy's growth trajectory. The company's ability to replicate these types of agreements with additional data center customers could further solidify its position in the clean energy market.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION (CEG) DEFIES MARKET TRENDS WITH POSITIVE GAINS

Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) ended the recent trading session at $292.77, reflecting a +1.95% change from the preceding day's closing price. This upward movement outpaced the S&P 500's daily loss of 0.41%. On the same day, the Dow experienced a decline of 0.36%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq saw a more substantial drop of 0.89%.Despite this daily gain, shares of Constellation Energy Corporation have depreciated by 5.33% over the past month, trailing the Oils-Energy sector's loss of 0.95% and the S&P 500's strong gain of 9.71%. Market analysts and investors are now pivoting their attention to the upcoming earnings disclosure for Constellation Energy, scheduled for May 11, 2026, with expectations pointing towards an earnings report of $2.52 per share. This figure would signify a year-over-year growth of 17.76%, while revenue forecasts suggest $8.77 billion, marking a significant 29.22% increase compared to the same quarter last year.Looking ahead, full-year projections from the Zacks Consensus Estimates indicate earnings of $11.78 per share and revenue of $32.63 billion for Constellation Energy Corporation, reflecting changes of +25.45% and +27.81%, respectively, from the prior year. Investors are advised to monitor any recent adjustments to analyst forecasts, as these revisions often indicate shifts in near-term business trends. Upward revisions in estimates typically signal a positive outlook on Constellation Energy's operational capabilities and profitability.To navigate these changes effectively, investors can leverage the Zacks Rank, a proprietary model that incorporates estimate changes to provide actionable ratings. The Zacks Rank system, which ranges from #1 (Strong Buy) to #5 (Strong Sell), has demonstrated an impressive track record of outperformance, with #1 ranked stocks yielding an average annual return of +25% since 1988. Over the last 30 days, the consensus EPS projection for Constellation Energy Corporation has increased by 1.32%, and the company currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).From a valuation perspective, Constellation Energy Corporation is trading at a Forward P/E ratio of 24.38, higher than the industry average of 16.55, suggesting a premium valuation. Additionally, CEG's PEG ratio stands at 1.13, considerably lower than the industry average of 2.52. The Alternative Energy - Other industry, part of the Oils-Energy sector, holds a favorable Zacks Industry Rank of 72, placing it among the top 30% of over 250 industries, indicating potential for future growth. Investors are encouraged to utilize Zacks.com for tracking stock-moving metrics and other relevant data in the upcoming trading sessions.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY FACES PRESSURE AS ANALYSTS ADJUST PRICE TARGETS

Shares of Constellation Energy (NASDAQ: CEG) are experiencing a notable decline today, driven by an analyst's recent downward adjustment of the stock's price target. As of 2:54 p.m. ET, Constellation Energy's stock is down 3.1%, continuing a downward trend that began after the company closed at $299.14 last Thursday. This decline follows the announcement made on 23rd April 2026 regarding the revised price forecast.Morgan Stanley, while maintaining a 'buy' rating on Constellation Energy, has lowered its price target to $360 from a previous $385. This adjustment has raised concerns among investors, although the new target still suggests a potential upside of approximately 25.2% from the stock's last closing price. The adjustment reflects a more cautious outlook but does not indicate a complete lack of confidence in the company's long-term prospects.Analysts have pointed out that while price targets can influence market sentiment, the fundamental performance of a company should be the primary focus for investors. Constellation Energy has been involved in nuclear energy, a sector that has seen fluctuating dynamics due to regulatory changes and the increasing demand for clean energy solutions. Investors are advised to weigh these factors alongside analyst projections when considering their positions in the stock.Despite the current volatility, some analysts remain optimistic about Constellation Energy's long-term growth potential. The company's commitment to expanding its renewable energy portfolio and reducing carbon emissions aligns with broader energy sector trends. This strategic direction could position Constellation Energy favorably as global energy policies increasingly favor sustainability.In conclusion, while the downward revision of the price target for Constellation Energy may cause short-term fluctuations in stock performance, investors are encouraged to consider the underlying fundamentals and industry trends. The current market reaction underscores the importance of a comprehensive analysis when making investment decisions in the energy sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ NRG ENERGY [3 News That Matters]

+ NRG ENERGY SECURES $2.6 BILLION THROUGH STRATEGIC DEBT OFFERINGS

HOUSTON - NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) has successfully priced a substantial $2.6 billion in debt offerings across three distinct tranches, as announced in a recent statement. This strategic move comes on the heels of the decision made on 17th April 2026 to enhance the company's liquidity and refinance existing obligations.The offerings comprise $500 million in 4.955% senior secured first lien notes due 2031, alongside $1.05 billion in 5.875% senior unsecured notes due 2034 and another $1.05 billion in 6.125% senior unsecured notes due 2036. The new notes will be guaranteed by NRG Energy's current and future wholly-owned U.S. subsidiaries, providing additional security for investors. The secured notes are backed by a first priority security interest in collateral already pledged for NRG's existing credit agreements and senior secured notes.As of the end of the fourth quarter of 2025, NRG Energy's total debt stood at $16.6 billion. However, the company maintains a robust current ratio of 1.64, indicating that its liquid assets comfortably exceed short-term obligations. The net proceeds from the current debt offerings will be utilized to repay a portion of outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility and fund the tender price for a previously announced offer by its subsidiary Lightning Power, LLC for outstanding senior secured notes. Any residual proceeds may be allocated for general corporate purposes, including the repayment of other debts.The completion of each of the offerings is independent, with no conditions tied to the other offerings or the term loan B. The notes are being offered exclusively to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A and to non-U.S. persons outside the United States under Regulation S, with no registration under the Securities Act of 1933. This move highlights NRG Energy's commitment to optimizing its capital structure amidst a dynamic market environment.In related news, NRG Energy's subsidiary Lightning Power has initiated a tender offer to purchase up to $1.5 billion in senior secured notes due 2032, offering $1,063.75 per $1,000 principal amount for notes tendered by 27th April 2026, including an early tender payment. Analysts have responded positively to NRG's strategic maneuvers, with Jefferies raising its price target to $199 and Wolfe Research upgrading the stock to Outperform, both citing the anticipated growth in the sector. Goldman Sachs has also reinstated coverage with a Buy rating, emphasizing the positive impact of NRG's recent acquisitions, which are expected to bolster its business profile moving forward.

+ NRG ENERGY UNVEILS NEW OFFERINGS OF SECURED AND UNSECURED NOTES

NRG Energy, Inc. has announced the commencement of concurrent offerings of senior secured notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036. On 14th April 2026, the company revealed that these notes will be guaranteed by its U.S. subsidiaries, with the secured notes backed by a first-priority lien on substantially all assets pledged under its existing credit facilities.The proceeds from these offerings are intended to be utilized in conjunction with a proposed $900 million term loan B, aimed primarily at repaying borrowings under NRG Energy's revolving credit facility. Additionally, the funds will support a tender offer for Lightning Power’s 7.25% senior secured notes, which are due in 2032. NRG Energy emphasizes that any remaining proceeds will be allocated towards paying fees and expenses, as well as general corporate purposes, including potential debt repayment.The announcement has been positively received in the market, as evidenced by a 3.76% increase in NRG Energy's shares following the news. Investors are keenly watching the company's financial maneuvers, particularly as it seeks to optimize its capital structure and enhance liquidity through these note offerings.This strategic move comes at a time when NRG Energy is focusing on strengthening its balance sheet and positioning itself for future growth opportunities. The successful execution of these offerings could provide the company with the necessary resources to manage its debt effectively while pursuing its operational goals.As NRG Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy landscape, the market will be attentive to how these financial strategies play out. Investors are encouraged to monitor the company's developments closely, as the implications of this notes offering could significantly impact its long-term financial health.

+ NRG ENERGY TARGETS DEBT MARKETS WITH NEW OFFERINGS AND $900 MILLION LOAN

NRG Energy, Inc. is making significant strides in the debt markets with a multi-tranche offering aimed at refinancing existing obligations and extending maturities. On 14th April 2026, NRG Energy announced the launch of concurrent offerings of senior secured first-lien notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036.The proceeds from these offerings, along with a new $900 million term loan B, will primarily be utilized to repay borrowings under NRG’s revolving credit facility and to fund a tender offer for outstanding 7.25% senior secured notes due 2032, which were issued by its subsidiary Lightning Power LLC. Additional funds may be allocated for general corporate purposes, including further debt reduction.The secured notes will be backed by a first-priority lien on a substantial portion of NRG's assets, mirroring collateral pledged under its existing credit facilities and secured notes. In contrast, the unsecured notes will rank behind the secured debt, offering investors a higher yield in exchange for increased risk — a choice that reflects the current appetite for yield among fixed-income investors.This transaction is indicative of a broader refinancing trend among U.S. independent power producers, many of whom are seeking to term out near-dated liabilities and lower funding costs following a period of elevated interest rates. The energy sector is undergoing a transformation as companies like NRG Energy position themselves for a surge in electricity demand driven by burgeoning data centers and electrification initiatives.As NRG Energy continues to navigate these dynamic market conditions, its latest debt offerings could attract fresh investor interest, particularly as power infrastructure becomes increasingly critical in the age of artificial intelligence. The steps taken by NRG demonstrate a proactive approach in optimizing its capital structure and extending financial flexibility.

value.Jurisdiction: California; Florida; Louisiana; New York
value.Title: Why this Louisiana military base spent $30 million to run on geothermal energy

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-69V3-RVX7-44C9-00000-00Why this Louisiana military base spent $30 million to run on geothermal energy2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:43ZLexisNexisWhy this Louisiana military base spent $30 million to run on geothermal energyPatrick Sisson

Given the rhetoric coming from today’s military leaders, you’d be right to think climate change and sustainability has been tossed aside. The nation’s 2025 National Security Strategy labeled climate change a “disastrous” ideology. “The Defense Department is not in the business of climate change, solving the global thermostat. We’re in the business of deterring and winning wars,” said Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth. 

And yet, there is still progress on sustainability being made; only now, it’s been rebranded as resiliency. At an Army base at Fort Polk in Louisiana, a renovation promises a cleaner, less carbon-intensive future, as well as a better living situation for servicemembers and their families. 

Completed in early March, the base represents a first-of-its-kind, $30 million investment in modernizing traditionally outdated and poorly maintained housing. It includes the installation of a large-scale geothermal energy system, all using U.S.-made equipment. It’s the first such geothermal installation at a U.S. military base, and an investment in reducing the installation’s carbon footprint. 

“What they get out of it is a much more efficient system that responds to their needs a lot better,” says John Plack, Senior Vice President of Engineering and Implementation at Ameresco, the contractor that oversaw the retrofit. “We’re directly eliminating fossil fuel for heating.”

[Photo: courtesy Ameresco]



Now, the 3,600 homes on base will see their energy bills slashed by 30%. The upgrades are projected to reduce the Fort Polk family housing portfolio’s annual electrical consumption and deliver more than $2.6 million in annual utility and operational cost savings. Beyond delivering long-term savings to the installation, the initiative fostered local economic growth by investing in the community and supporting the local workforce. 

Resilient housing on the rise

The military has a lengthy history of making substantial investment in more sustainable and resilient buildings and housing, even during the current administration. As far back as 2003, during the George W. Bush administration, the Department of Defense was commissioning reports on climate risks. 

Typically it has viewed this issue through the lens of resiliency: adapting bases to rising sea levels, making installations more self-sufficient, and cutting the military’s massive reliance on unstable oil supplies. 

Right now, Tyndall Air Force Base in Panama City, Florida, is being rebuilt as a demonstration of resilient military construction, in response to a 2018 storm that caused $5 billion in damages. New buildings rest more than a foot above the ground, roofs have been hardened to survive 165–mile-per-hour winds, and a manmade oyster reef offshore is meant to break up waves. 

It’s one of a number of resiliency-focused projects that have continued under the current administration. The military has continued this work set in motion during previous administrations, despite policy shifts coming from the White House and Pentagon, for resilience and budget reasons. A Bloomberg Law analysis found that roughly $400 billion in federal assets, mostly in the Defense Department, stand at risk of a major flood or storm.

Ameresco, which does a lot of federal work with groups like Veterans Affairs or the General Services Administration, built Fort Polk’s geothermal system by taking advantage of a program called ESPC, or Energy Savings Performance Contracts. 

Enacted as part of the Energy Policy Act of 1992, it’s a way to invest in energy performance/efficiency upgrades on federal property without specific congressional appropriations, as long as there’s no upfront capital costs. The funding is structured as such that the base pays the private contractor over 25 years via the energy savings achieved by the installation. 

So when a contractor like Ameresco comes in—bringing drill rigs into the backyard, trenching around utilities, and running piping through buildings—they’re paid as a down payment on future energy systems. 

According to the Army’s own reporting, it’s a great way to upgrade outdated military and other government facilities without spending government money, and it tends to exceed projected savings. It’s a massive program—the latest batch of funding for these kinds of projects, ESPC IV, was worth $3 billion—and Ameresco is working on a number of other, similar programs, including an array of energy-saving additions including solar and battery storage. 

Plack says that over the last few decades Ameresco has been doing this work, the projects have become more sophisticated, as technology has improved. A recently finished project in Petaluma, California, retrofitted the Coast Guard training facility to be more resilient in the event of a grid failure; a new microgrid, with solar panels and a storage battery, was added in response to a previous fire that left the installation without power for five days. When it comes to the Armed Forces and sustainability, practicality tends to be a consistent objective. 

“We’re able to be very flexible with the technologies that fit the specific base or use case,” says Plack. “We’re pretty technology agnostic. It’s really what fits best.”

value.Jurisdiction: Florida; International
value.Title: ELEKTROS Inc. Positions Investors at a Rare Ground-Floor Entry Point Into the Global Lithium Mining and EV Infrastructure Boom

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJD-V8T3-S1XN-53JD-00000-00ELEKTROS Inc. Positions Investors at a Rare Ground-Floor Entry Point Into the Global Lithium Mining and EV Infrastructure Boom2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:42ZLexisNexisELEKTROS Inc. Positions Investors at a Rare Ground-Floor Entry Point Into the Global Lithium Mining and EV Infrastructure Boom

ELEKTROS Inc. - Publicly Traded (Ticker Symbol:ELEK)

WEST PALM BEACH, FL / ACCESS Newswire / May 10, 2026 / As U.S. stock markets continue reaching historic all-time highs and investor appetite for next-generation energy and technology opportunities accelerates across Wall Street, ELEKTROS Inc. (Ticker Symbol:ELEK) announced today that the Company continues aggressively advancing its lithium mining initiatives, electric vehicle charging patent strategy, and broader electrification-focused growth initiatives.

Management believes the global electrification revolution remains in its early innings, particularly as demand for electric vehicles, battery storage systems, and rapid charging infrastructure continues expanding worldwide. The Company further believes that current market conditions may provide investors with an opportunity to participate in the emerging lithium and EV infrastructure sectors at what management considers a ground-floor valuation relative to long-term industry potential.

ELEKTROS continues focusing on hard rock lithium exploration and development opportunities in Sierra Leone, Africa, positioning the Company within one of the world's most strategically important supply chains supporting electric vehicles, renewable energy storage, and industrial electrification.

The importance of lithium to the future of transportation and clean energy has been repeatedly highlighted by industry leaders worldwide. Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla, previously stated:

"It is definitely true that the fundamental enabling technology for electric cars is lithium-ion as a cell chemistry technology. In the absence of that, I don't think it's possible to make an electric car that is competitive with a gasoline car."

In addition, Musk has publicly emphasized the importance of expanding charging infrastructure and improving charging efficiency to support mass EV adoption globally.

In parallel with its lithium initiatives, ELEKTROS holds United States Patent No. 12,522,100 B1 related to advanced electric vehicle charging technology designed to significantly reduce charging times and improve charging efficiency for EV users globally.

The Company believes that fast-charging infrastructure may become one of the defining components of the next generation EV ecosystem as consumers increasingly demand reduced charging times, improved convenience, and enhanced vehicle usability.

ELEKTROS further announced that on May 5, 2026, the Company delivered a cease-and-desist notice regarding activities involving a major global automotive manufacturer whose identity the Company has elected not to publicly disclose at this time concerning matters related to potential infringement involving ELEKTROS' patented charging technology.

Management stated that the protection of shareholder value and the defense of intellectual property rights remain core priorities. The Company indicated that it continues actively evaluating potential unauthorized use of its patented technology throughout the electric vehicle industry.

ELEKTROS also confirmed that management expects to initiate strategic communications involving potential licensing discussions, including possible one-year lease arrangements related to the patented technology, as well as broader strategic alternatives that may include acquisition or buyout proposals concerning the Company's patent portfolio.

The growing importance of lithium and rare earth minerals continues drawing increasing attention across global financial markets. Major financial publications, including The Wall Street Journal, have extensively reported on the strategic importance of critical minerals, lithium supply chains, and rare earth resource development as nations race to secure the raw materials necessary for the future of electric transportation, artificial intelligence infrastructure, defense technologies, and energy independence.

Management believes that successful monetization, licensing, or strategic acquisition of the Company's patent portfolio could represent a highly significant event for the Company and its shareholders, potentially creating substantial long-term enterprise value.

"This patent represents years of innovation, engineering, persistence, and commitment," said Shlomo Bleier, CEO of ELEKTROS Inc. "We believe our technology has the potential to redefine the electric vehicle charging experience globally while positioning ELEKTROS at the center of one of the largest industrial transformations of our generation. At the same time, we remain deeply committed to responsibly advancing our lithium initiatives while aggressively protecting the intellectual property we have worked tirelessly to build."

ELEKTROS stated that management continues working aggressively across all operational divisions with the objective of building a globally recognized energy and technology company positioned at the intersection of lithium resources, electrification, and EV infrastructure innovation.

Patent Reference:
United States Patent No. 12,522,100 B1
Patent Link: https://patents.google.com/patent/US12522100B1

Forward-Looking Statements:
This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of applicable securities laws. These statements involve risks, uncertainties, and assumptions that may cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied. Statements regarding patent enforcement, licensing opportunities, potential acquisitions, market opportunities, shareholder value, operational execution, lithium exploration activities, and future strategic growth are forward-looking in nature and subject to numerous risks and uncertainties. Investors are encouraged to conduct their own independent due diligence before making any investment decisions.

Contact Information:
ELEKTROS Inc.
Publicly Traded (Ticker Symbol:ELEK)
Phone: 786-477-9003
Email: elektrosinc@gmail.com
Website: www.elektros.energy

ELEKTROS Inc. - Publicly Traded (Ticker Symbol:ELEK)

Management believes current market momentum reflects the powerful investor excitement seen during the early Dot-Com era, with rising energy prices, expanding EV demand, data center growth, and surging global power consumption potentially creating what management describes as an exceptional long-term opportunity for early investors.

As U.S. stock markets continue rallying toward historic highs, management believes ELEKTROS may represent a rare bottom-basement discount opportunity similar to the early dot-com boom of the 1990s.

West Palm Beach, Florida

SOURCE: Elektros, Inc.

value.Title: ELEKTROS Inc. Positions Investors at the Ground Floor of the Global Lithium and EV Infrastructure Boom as U.S. Stock Markets Reach Historic Highs - Company Advances High-Value Patent Monetization Strategy and Strategic Lithium Expansion Opportunities

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-WRN3-S30S-H541-00000-00ELEKTROS Inc. Positions Investors at the Ground Floor of the Global Lithium and EV Infrastructure Boom as U.S. Stock Markets Reach Historic Highs - Company Advances High-Value Patent Monetization Strategy and Strategic Lithium Expansion Opportunities2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:42ZLexisNexisELEKTROS Inc. Positions Investors at the Ground Floor of the Global Lithium and EV Infrastructure Boom as U.S. Stock Markets Reach Historic Highs - Company Advances High-Value Patent Monetization Strategy and Strategic Lithium Expansion Opportunities

ELEKTROS Inc. - Publicly Traded (Ticker Symbol: ELEK)

WEST PALM BEACH, FL / ACCESS Newswire / May 8, 2026 / As U.S. stock markets continue reaching historic all-time highs and investor appetite for next-generation energy and technology opportunities accelerates across Wall Street, ELEKTROS Inc. (Ticker Symbol:ELEK) announced today that the Company continues aggressively advancing its lithium mining initiatives, electric vehicle charging patent strategy, and broader electrification-focused growth initiatives.

Management believes the global electrification revolution remains in its early innings, particularly as demand for electric vehicles, battery storage systems, and rapid charging infrastructure continues expanding worldwide. The Company further believes that current market conditions may provide investors with an opportunity to participate in the emerging lithium and EV infrastructure sectors at what management considers a ground-floor valuation relative to long-term industry potential.

ELEKTROS continues focusing on hard rock lithium exploration and development opportunities in Sierra Leone, Africa, positioning the Company within one of the world's most strategically important supply chains supporting electric vehicles, renewable energy storage, and industrial electrification.

The importance of lithium to the future of transportation and clean energy has been repeatedly highlighted by industry leaders worldwide. Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla, previously stated:

"It is definitely true that the fundamental enabling technology for electric cars is lithium-ion as a cell chemistry technology. In the absence of that, I don't think it's possible to make an electric car that is competitive with a gasoline car."

In addition, Musk has publicly emphasized the importance of expanding charging infrastructure and improving charging efficiency to support mass EV adoption globally.

In parallel with its lithium initiatives, ELEKTROS holds United States Patent No. 12,522,100 B1 related to advanced electric vehicle charging technology designed to significantly reduce charging times and improve charging efficiency for EV users globally.

The Company believes that fast-charging infrastructure may become one of the defining components of the next generation EV ecosystem as consumers increasingly demand reduced charging times, improved convenience, and enhanced vehicle usability.

ELEKTROS further announced that on May 5, 2026, the Company delivered a cease-and-desist notice regarding activities involving a major global automotive manufacturer whose identity the Company has elected not to publicly disclose at this time concerning matters related to potential infringement involving ELEKTROS' patented charging technology.

Management stated that the protection of shareholder value and the defense of intellectual property rights remain core priorities. The Company indicated that it continues actively evaluating potential unauthorized use of its patented technology throughout the electric vehicle industry.

ELEKTROS also confirmed that management expects to initiate strategic communications involving potential licensing discussions, including possible one-year lease arrangements related to the patented technology, as well as broader strategic alternatives that may include acquisition or buyout proposals concerning the Company's patent portfolio.

The growing importance of lithium and rare earth minerals continues drawing increasing attention across global financial markets. Major financial publications, including The Wall Street Journal, have extensively reported on the strategic importance of critical minerals, lithium supply chains, and rare earth resource development as nations race to secure the raw materials necessary for the future of electric transportation, artificial intelligence infrastructure, defense technologies, and energy independence.

Management believes that successful monetization, licensing, or strategic acquisition of the Company's patent portfolio could represent a highly significant event for the Company and its shareholders, potentially creating substantial long-term enterprise value.

"This patent represents years of innovation, engineering, persistence, and commitment," said Shlomo Bleier, CEO of ELEKTROS Inc. "We believe our technology has the potential to redefine the electric vehicle charging experience globally while positioning ELEKTROS at the center of one of the largest industrial transformations of our generation. At the same time, we remain deeply committed to responsibly advancing our lithium initiatives while aggressively protecting the intellectual property we have worked tirelessly to build."

ELEKTROS stated that management continues working aggressively across all operational divisions with the objective of building a globally recognized energy and technology company positioned at the intersection of lithium resources, electrification, and EV infrastructure innovation.

Patent Reference:
United States Patent No. 12,522,100 B1
Patent Link: https://patents.google.com/patent/US12522100B1

Forward-Looking Statements:
This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of applicable securities laws. These statements involve risks, uncertainties, and assumptions that may cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied. Statements regarding patent enforcement, licensing opportunities, potential acquisitions, market opportunities, shareholder value, operational execution, lithium exploration activities, and future strategic growth are forward-looking in nature and subject to numerous risks and uncertainties. Investors are encouraged to conduct their own independent due diligence before making any investment decisions.

value.Title: OXBOW - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-WBY3-S0GM-T18V-00000-00OXBOW - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:43ZLexisNexisSTOCKOXBOW - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: OXBOW

PermID4296883232Websitehttps://www.oxbow.com/IndustryOil and Gas

ACTIVITIES:

Oxbow Corporation, founded in 1983 by William I. Koch in West Palm Beach, Florida, is a privately held energy and commodities company. Specializing in petroleum coke, coal, and carbon products, Oxbow generates over $4 billion annually, operating in 40 countries. With 1,200 employees, its subsidiaries, like Oxbow Carbon, focus on calcined coke for aluminum and steel industries. Oxbows logistics network ensures efficient global distribution. The company invests in renewable energy and carbon management, aligning with sustainability trends. Despite legal disputes, including a $1 billion tax settlement in 2021, Oxbow remains a key player in energy markets, leveraging innovation and strategic partnerships to drive growth.

+ OXBOW [3 News That Matters]

+ BRI WARNER TAKES THE HELM AT OXBOW, FREEING TIM ADAMS TO FOCUS ON BRAND EXPANSION

Oxbow Brewing Company is undergoing a leadership transition as Bri Warner, the new president, takes a more active role in the brewery’s day-to-day operations. As of 18th March 2026, Warner is two months into her position, allowing founder Tim Adams to concentrate on promoting Oxbow and its recently acquired brand, Rising Tide.Warner, who previously worked at Atlantic Sea Farms, the largest kelp farming operation in the United States, brings a fresh perspective to Oxbow. In a podcast recorded at Oxbow’s Portland taproom, Adams expressed his confidence in Warner’s capabilities, stating, "Bri just is an absolute master at doing that."With Warner at the helm of operations, Adams has been able to step back from daily management and focus on brand representation, including a winter stint at Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford, Maine, engaging with trade partners and consumers while overseeing the brewery’s offerings.Warner noted that this arrangement helps to free Tim to grow the brand in a passionate way, adding, "What that does is it keeps Tim from being out on the road, and I think probably the most power of this is the fact that we get to free him to do the work to continue to grow the brand in a really great and passionate way."The leadership change comes as breweries navigate balancing operational demands with brand growth. Warner’s goal is to tighten internal operations while preserving Oxbow’s commitment to quality and creativity, a move that industry observers say could support multi-brand expansion across locations. The conversation also touched on broader beer-market moves, including Tilray’s acquisition of BrewDog’s Australian operations and the launch of George Clooney’s new non-alcoholic brand.

+ CHARLEMONT'S OXBOW RESORT EXTENDS OPERATIONS THROUGH BERKSHIRE PIZZERIA BUY AND OTTERS PROPERTY

Oxbow Resort owner Travis DeCere is broadening his Charlemont portfolio by purchasing Berkshire Pizzeria and the former Otters restaurant. The pizzeria sold for $330,000 through Berkshire Equity LLC and was finalized on 8th February 2026, as DeCere moves to diversify his offerings while the Oxbow Resort Motel work continues.DeCere's frequent trips to Charlemont led him to reconnect with Greg Rowehl, the pizzeria's previous owner, and he hatched the plan with his partner Colleen Brady to take on both properties.Brady relocated from Florida and brings a background in operating food trucks. She intends to expand the pizzeria menu to include fresh chicken tenders and possibly pasta, and to introduce a full bar on the back deck. The pair hope extended hours and a refreshed ambiance will draw more patrons, including skiers from Berkshire East Mountain Resort.The deal also includes the purchase of the Otters site at 1745 Route 2 for $250,000, with plans to find a tenant who can run a full-service restaurant there.Together with ongoing renovations and a push to create a community hub, the projects reflect DeCere and Brady's vision to honor the Rowehl legacy while expanding Charlemont's dining options.

+ BRIANA WARNER APPOINTED PRESIDENT OF OXBOW BREWING COMPANY

PORTLAND, MAINE — Oxbow Brewing Company announced on Feb. 11, 2026 that Briana Warner has been named President. Warner previously served as CEO of Atlantic Sea Farms and has a background as a U.S. diplomat, bringing a track record of scaling mission-driven businesses while maintaining community focus. In her new role, she will oversee company strategy, daily operations, and growth across Oxbow's wholesale and hospitality channels.In her expanded duties, Warner will work with the leadership team to navigate Oxbow's ongoing regional expansion while preserving the brewery’s craft and place-based ethos. Tim Adams, the company’s founder, will remain actively involved in brand development, creative direction, sales, and community engagement. 'Briana is an outstanding leader and a natural fit for Oxbow,' Adams said, noting that her experience and values align with thoughtful, sustainable growth.Oxbow operates two taprooms in Portland and the Oxbow Beer Garden in Oxford, a year-round destination that pairs wood-fired, farm-driven food with outdoor recreation. The Oxford campus features winter trails and an 18-hole disc golf course, underscoring the brewery’s community orientation and seasonal menus built around on-site organic produce. The company’s recent acquisition of Rising Tide Brewing Company has expanded production capacity and strengthened its Maine craft portfolio while keeping both brands independently owned. Warner’s appointment is a key step toward long-term stability and continued growth for Oxbow and Rising Tide.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY (NASDAQ:CEG) PUSHES FOR REGULATORY PERMISSION TO RESTART CRANE NUCLEAR PLANT BY NEXT YEAR

NEW YORK, May 11 (Reuters) - Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) says regulators could issue a decision as early as next month that will determine when its Crane Clean Energy Center, formerly Three Mile Island, can restart. The company aims to revive the Pennsylvania nuclear plant by next year and has asked the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to allow a timing workaround for the restart. The date of this report is 11th May 2026.Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) notes that PJM grid feedback suggests the plant may not deliver power to the grid until 2031, underscoring timeline uncertainty. FERC is expected to decide in June or July on Constellation's request to transfer some rights to inject electricity from its Eddystone natural gas-fired generating station to Crane, a shift that could adjust the restart timetable.The plan centers on a contract to supply power to Microsoft’s regional data centers, highlighting growing demand for energy tied to data-intensive facilities. The company said the move to transfer injection rights could unlock earlier operation if approved.CEO Joe Dominguez said on a quarterly earnings call that the team is pursuing speed and clarity, adding that the plant's restart should not be viewed as impossible in the near term. Historically, no fully shut power plant has been restarted in the United States, but three revival efforts are underway, and Crane’s progress will inform the broader trajectory for nuclear energy amid electrification.

+ CONSTELLATION ENERGY (NASDAQ:CEG) POSTS CAUTIOUS Q1 OUTLOOK AS ANALYSTS TRIM TARGETS

Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) has been highlighted as one of the 12 Best Electric Utility Stocks to Buy for expected data center investment growth. The emphasis on clean energy aligns with technology users seeking reliable, low-emission powerOn 11th May 2026, Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) will disclose its Q1 results, with analysts expecting a stable performance. TD Cowen moved its price target from $390 to $381 while preserving a Buy rating, implying upside of more than 22% from current levels. Earlier, on 29th April, Scotiabank analyst Andrew Weisel lowered his target but maintained an Outperform rating, reflecting a cautious near-term view due to modest earnings growth driven by capacity prices. Looking forward, Constellation Energy targets adjusted earnings of $11 to $12 per share for fiscal 2026, and expects base EPS growth of 20% or more from 2026 through 2029. While the stock presents a disciplined growth narrative tied to sustainable energy, some analysts see AI sectors offering different upside potential with lower risk

+ WEATHERFORD INTERNATIONAL PLC (NASDAQ: WFRD) SECURES DEEPWATER WORK AND DRILLING SUPPORT FOR CONSTELLATION OIL SERVICES IN BRAZIL

Constellation Oil Services awards Weatherford International plc (NASDAQ: WFRD) two offshore contracts to bolster Brazil's deepwater program, reinforcing Weatherford's footprint in the region. The Gold Star contract expands Weatherford's offerings on Constellation's ultra-deepwater semisubmersible Gold Star, operating in the Campos and Espirito Santo Basins, delivering plug and abandonment and workover services through December 2028, with services integrated directly through the rig. Under the Brava Star campaign, Weatherford will provide advanced drilling technology for Constellation's drillship Brava Star, set to commence in the first quarter of 2027 and continue through December 2030. This follows the date of 6th May 2026. Girish Saligram, President and CEO of Weatherford, says the awards demonstrate Weatherford's ability to conduct complex offshore operations through a combination of proven technology and robust local delivery, improving safety and performance. The agreements highlight Weatherford's offshore technology portfolio and its robust in-country support, positioning the company as a preferred partner as Constellation advances its Brazil offshore program in the Campos, Espirito Santo, and Buzios basins.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ NRG ENERGY [3 News That Matters]

+ NRG ENERGY, INC. (NYSE:NRG) SECURES $2.6 BILLION THROUGH SENIOR SECURED AND UNSECURED NOTE ISSUANCES

On Apr. 14, 2026, NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) priced $2.6 billion in senior secured and unsecured notes. The issue includes $500 million of 4.955% secured notes due 2031. It also issues $1.05 billion in unsecured notes with 5.875% due 2034 and 6.125% due 2036. The company plans to use the proceeds to repay existing debt, fund a tender offer for certain notes, and support general corporate purposes. NRG also proposed a $900 million term loan to augment liquidity.

+ NRG ENERGY, INC. (NYSE:NRG) SETS CONCURRENT NOTES OFFERS TO SUPPORT DEBT REFINANCING

On 14th April 2026, NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) Reports concurrent offerings of senior secured notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036. The notes will be guaranteed by its U.S. subsidiaries, and the secured notes will be backed by a first-priority lien on substantially all assets pledged under its existing credit facilities.Proceeds will be used in conjunction with a proposed $900 million term loan B to repay borrowings under the revolving credit facility. The funds will also support a tender offer for Lightning Power’s 7.25% senior secured notes due 2032.Any remaining proceeds will be allocated to fees and expenses and general corporate purposes, including debt repayment.NRG Energy’s shares rose 3.76% after the news, underscoring investor interest in the plan and the company’s balance-sheet actions.The actions align with the firm’s objective to optimize its capital structure and liquidity to pursue growth opportunities

+ NRG ENERGY, INC. (NYSE:NRG) SETS UP REFINANCING WITH SENIOR SECURED AND UNSECURED NOTES AND A $900 MILLION TERM LOAN

On 14th April 2026, NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) Sets up a refinancing plan through concurrent offerings of senior secured first-lien notes due 2031 and senior unsecured notes due 2034 and 2036, along with a new $900 million term loan B. Proceeds will fund repayments of revolver borrowings and a tender offer for Lightning Power LLC's outstanding 7.25% senior secured notes due 2032, with additional funds available for general corporate purposes, including further debt reduction.The secured notes will be secured by a first-priority lien on a substantial portion of NRG's assets, consistent with collateral pledged under existing facilities and notes. The unsecured notes will rank behind the secured debt, providing higher yields to investors to reflect increased risk.The deal illustrates a broader trend among U.S. independent power producers to extend near-term maturities and reduce funding costs in a higher-rate environment. NRG notes that power infrastructure demand is rising amid data-center expansion and electrification, positioning the company to manage liquidity while preserving financial flexibility.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

KLX Inc is a specialized provider of aerospace and energy services, with operations centered in the German market. The company focuses on delivering mission-critical components, including fasteners and consumables, as well as maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) services to commercial and defense sectors. Its core activities encompass the distribution of high-reliability parts and comprehensive support for aircraft and energy infrastructure, emphasizing efficiency and innovation in supply chain management. The fastest-growing segments include advanced MRO solutions for commercial aviation, fueled by rising global travel demands, and expanding energy services in sustainable technologies, such as renewable energy maintenance. Through strategic partnerships and technological advancements, KLX Inc maintains a competitive edge, supporting key industries with reliable, high-performance offerings. It is Germany's 200th largest Total German Market ex DAX Index company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: XCF Global Continues New Rise Reno Planned Upgrade and Secures Forbearance Agreement Related to New Rise Renewables Reno Ground Lease

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-J2M3-RYJ3-74FN-00000-00XCF Global Continues New Rise Reno Planned Upgrade and Secures Forbearance Agreement Related to New Rise Renewables Reno Ground Lease2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:42ZLexisNexisXCF Global Continues New Rise Reno Planned Upgrade and Secures Forbearance Agreement Related to New Rise Renewables Reno Ground Lease

Agreement continues through January 1, 2027, subject to specified conditions, supporting New Rise's planned upgrade path and operational progress.

HOUSTON, TX / ACCESS Newswire / May 1, 2026 / XCF Global, Inc. ("XCF") (NASDAQ:SAFX) an emerging player in lowering emissions and strengthening domestic renewable energy resilience of the aviation industry through Sustainable Aviation Fuel ("SAF"), today announced that New Rise Renewables Reno, LLC ("New Rise"), a subsidiary of XCF, has entered into a forbearance agreement dated April 27, 2026 (the "Agreement") with Twain GL XXVIII, LLC (the "Landlord") in connection with the ground lease for New Rise's Reno, Nevada facility.

New Rise Reno was commissioned in February 2025 and has produced SAF, renewable diesel, and renewable naphtha. Since the start of commercial operations in March 2025, the facility has produced more than 2.5 million gallons of renewable fuels. New Rise Reno is in the final stages of its planned upgrade, intended to strengthen long-term operability and repeatability. Current workstreams are focused on improving operating stability and equipment readiness, reinforcing quality systems required for certified fuel, and strengthening start-up and operating procedures. XCF is targeting a return to operations in June, subject to completion of upgrade activities and standard start-up procedures.

Under the Agreement, the Landlord agreed to forbear from exercising certain rights and remedies under the applicable lease documents with respect to certain alleged events of default through January 1, 2027, subject to New Rise's compliance with the terms and conditions of the Agreement including the required payments therein. For more information, please see the form of agreement filed today on Form 8-k.

About XCF Global, Inc.
XCF Global, Inc. ("XCF") is an emerging sustainable aviation fuel company dedicated to accelerating the aviation industry's transition to net-zero emissions. Our flagship facility, New Rise Renewables Reno, has a permitted nameplate production capacity of 38 million gallons per year, positioning XCF as an early mover among large-scale SAF producers in North America. XCF is working to advance a pipeline of potential expansion opportunities in Nevada, North Carolina, and Florida, and to build partnerships across the energy and transportation sectors to scale SAF globally. XCF is listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market and trades under the ticker, SAFX.

To learn more, visit www.xcf.global

Contacts

XCF Global: Corporate Comms
media@xcf.global

value.Title: MasTec to Host Investor Day in New York City Today at 8:45 AM ET

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJN-3P03-RRRB-M3RH-00000-00MasTec to Host Investor Day in New York City Today at 8:45 AM ET2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:42ZLexisNexisMasTec to Host Investor Day in New York City Today at 8:45 AM ETCORAL GABLES, Fla.

MasTec, Inc. (NYSE: MTZ) will host an in-person, invitation-only, Investor Day in New York City today beginning at 8:45 a.m. ET, as previously announced. The event is expected to conclude at approximately 12:00 p.m. ET.

Presentations will be made by members of MasTec's Senior and Operational Management Teams, providing an in-depth review of MasTec's business strategy, growth drivers and multi-year financial targets. The session will also include a question and answer session with the various presenters.

A live webcast of the presentation, along with the presentation materials, will be accessible on www.mastec.com on the "Events & Presentations" section of the website's Investors drop-down tab. A replay of the event will also be available on the website shortly following the conclusion of the event.

About MasTec

MasTec, Inc. is a leading North American infrastructure engineering and construction company focused primarily on engineering, building, installation, maintenance and upgrade of communications, energy and utility and other infrastructure. MasTec primarily operates under four business segments including Communications, serving both wireless and wireline/fiber infrastructure; Power Delivery, serving primarily utility customers in transmission and distribution markets; Pipeline Infrastructure serving energy and other customers with installation and maintenance services primarily for natural gas pipeline and distribution infrastructure; and Clean Energy and Infrastructure, providing renewable energy engineering and construction services, as well as for heavy civil and other industrial infrastructure markets. Learn more at www.mastec.com .

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260512341853/en/



CONTACT: J. Marc Lewis, Investor Relations
305-406-1815
marc.lewis@mastec.com
